news.Title: 川普以荷莫茲海峽賭戰 台股跌逾800點新台幣貶破32元
news.Content:
美國總統川普放話，如伊朗在48小時內，拒絕開放荷莫茲海峽，美方就會摧毀伊朗的能源設施。伊朗則反過頭警告，如果美國敢有動作，會收緊海峽的通行。川普以荷莫茲海峽開放作為威脅，最後通牒期限倒數，擔憂中東戰事升級，亞洲主要股市今天(23日)全收黑，台北股市終場大跌逾800點，跌幅2.45%，收32722點。新台幣兌美元匯價收盤也貶破32元大關。

美國總統川普在美東時間21日晚間近8時，也就是台灣時間22日上午近8時，對伊朗下達48小時通牒，要求重新開放荷莫茲海峽。伊朗強硬拒絕，不僅揚言報復美國及其中東盟友，還警告將徹底封鎖荷莫茲海峽。

川普以荷莫茲海峽開放作為威脅，最後通牒期限倒數，也讓全球股市再度陷入「愁雲慘霧」。亞洲主要股市23日一開盤就下殺，韓國股市終場跌幅高達逾6%，日本股市跌幅也逾3%，台北股市權值股台積電、鴻海以及聯發科，再度成為外資提款機，大盤指數終場下跌821點，跌幅2.45%，收32722點，失守33000點大關。分析師龔鴻彬說：『(原音)外資的買賣超其實很被動，被動就是以匯率的走勢為主，那匯率的走勢又受到美元指數，相對應的會走弱。所以你可以看到今天新台幣走勢的貶值幅度相對比較大。那自然而然外資，尤其是被動型的外資，就必須得去調節手上台股的現貨持股，加大期貨空倉的力道。』

觀察三大法人23日在台股買賣超情況，合計共賣超1046億元，其中外資大砍839.22億，創史上第十大賣超紀錄；累計3月以來，外資賣超已經高達8510億。

美伊戰事持續升溫，市場避險情緒升高，資金持續回流美元。在外資匯出下，新台幣兌美元匯價，23日一度重貶逾2角，來到最高32.196元價位，尾盤央行進場調節，貶幅收斂，終場貶值1.22角，收32.902元，貶破32元大關，創下去年5月初以來新低。(編輯：宋皖媛)
news.AI_Summary:
• 美國總統川普對伊朗發出最後通牒，擔憂中東戰事升級，亞洲主要股市23日全收黑。
• 台北股市終場大跌逾800點，跌幅2.45%，收32722點，失守33000點大關。
• 新台幣兌美元匯價收盤貶破32元大關，終場貶值1.22角，收32.902元。
• 三大法人23日合計賣超1046億元，外資大砍839.22億，創史上第十大賣超紀錄。
• 市場避險情緒升高，資金持續回流美元。
news.Keywords: 地緣政治, 避險情緒, 外資賣超, 匯率貶值, 股市重挫

news.Title: 外資連3週賣超台股破千億　上週減碼鴻海14.7萬張居首
news.Content:
（中央社記者曾仁凱台北2026年03月23日電）美伊戰火延燒，台股高檔震盪，上週五集中市場指數以33543.88點作收，週漲143.56點或0.43%，終止連2跌。不過根據台灣證券交易所統計，外資上週賣超新台幣1655.91億元，為連續第3週賣超破千億，合計3週賣超金額約7205億元。

個股方面，外資上週賣超前3名上市公司為鴻海 (2317) 14萬7546張、群創 (3481) 14萬4196張，和友達 (2409) 12萬8320張。

買超榜方面，外資上週買超前3名上市公司為大聯大 (3702) 5萬4695張、旺宏 (2337) 3萬8687張，和萬海 (2615) 3萬5825張。

另統計今年初至3月20日止，外資累計賣超金額為5857.85億元，外資總持有股票市值52兆6275億元，占全體上市股票市值比重48.14%。
news.AI_Summary:
• 外資上週賣超新台幣1655.91億元，為連續第3週賣超破千億，合計3週賣超金額約7205億元。
• 今年初至3月20日止，外資累計賣超金額為5857.85億元。
• 外資上週賣超前3名上市公司為鴻海、群創、友達。
• 外資上週買超前3名上市公司為大聯大、旺宏、萬海。
• 外資總持有股票市值52兆6275億元，占全體上市股票市值比重48.14%。
news.Keywords: 外資賣超, 累計賣超, 籌碼流向, 權值股調節, 避險

news.Title: 《韓股》韓股重挫逾6%觸發熔斷 韓元貶新低
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】23日，開低的韓國股市收盤時重挫，主要是中東危機持續惡化，加上美伊兩國在荷姆茲海峽的問題上仍在對峙，局勢絲毫沒有緩和的跡象。韓股KOSPI最後大幅下跌375.45點或6.49%，收5,405.75點。同時，韓元兌美元匯價也貶至十幾年以來最低，外資法人合計共賣超高達7.5兆韓元。

分析師指出，自從二月底美以聯手攻打伊朗之後，韓股波動劇烈，三月四日一度崩跌超過12%，隨後在下一個交易日五日又出現強勁反彈，但在接下來的一周，又大跌超過5%。韓股23日崩跌，韓國交易所曾暫停程式化交易五分鐘，已是今年第六次執行這種交易限制令。

韓國青瓦台表示，李在明總統計畫在23日召開並親自主持一般性的安全會議，評估韓國的安全情勢與因應措施。同時，韓國總統府也否認，國家安全顧問要訪問美國華府的媒體報導。不過，軍事專家指出，李在明連普通的安全會議都要參加，代表整個國際局勢，似乎正朝著另個險峻的方向在前進。

市值最高的三星電子重挫6.57%，記憶體晶片競爭對手SK海力士也大跌7.35%。另外，韓國最大的汽車製造商現代汽車下挫6.19%，軍工大廠韓華航空航太大跌3.18%，大型金融集團KB大跌6.38%，韓國最大船公司hmm也大跌6.78%。

韓元兌一美元匯價貶至17年來新低，也就是說，上次韓元重貶時，是出現在2009年3月9日全球金融海嘯期間，當時韓元一度貶至1549。目前，韓元在1511.64附近。
news.AI_Summary:
• 韓國股市23日重挫6.49%，收5,405.75點，觸發熔斷機制。
• 外資法人合計賣超高達7.5兆韓元。
• 韓元兌美元匯價貶至17年來新低，收盤價約在1511.64附近。
• 三星電子重挫6.57%，SK海力士大跌7.35%，現代汽車下挫6.19%。
• 韓國交易所曾暫停程式化交易五分鐘，為今年第六次執行交易限制令。
news.Keywords: 股市重挫, 外資賣超, 匯率貶值, 熔斷機制, 避險

news.Title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Stock Market Sell-Off? History Offers a Clear Answer.
news.Content:
Over 3,500 companies have chosen to go public through the Nasdaq stock exchange. The Nasdaq-100 index tracks the performance of the top 100 (by value) companies, excluding banks and other financial institutions.

Since the technology sector is home to more trillion-dollar companies than any other sector, it boasts a dominant weighting of almost 60% in the Nasdaq-100. That means companies at the cutting edge of industries like artificial intelligence (AI) have a significant influence over the performance of the index, which is why it typically delivers higher returns than more diversified indexes like the S&P 500 .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But that can also be a recipe for volatility. The Nasdaq-100 is currently trading down 8.8% from its all-time high amid rising economic uncertainty and geopolitical tensions, whereas the S&P 500 has declined by a lesser 7%.

The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ) is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) that tracks the performance of the Nasdaq-100 by holding the same stocks and maintaining similar weightings. Is the recent sell-off a buying opportunity for investors? Here's what history says.

Tech stocks tend to lead the market higher

The Nasdaq-100 (and by extension, the Invesco QQQ ETF) invests across 10 different economic sectors, but as I mentioned earlier, almost 60% of the value of its entire portfolio is parked in technology stocks. The tech sector is home to five companies valued at $1 trillion or more, and four of them are among the top holdings in the Nasdaq-100:

Nvidia: $4.2 trillion

Apple: $3.64 trillion

Microsoft: $2.84 trillion

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing: $1.71 trillion (not in the Nasdaq-100, because it's listed on the New York Stock Exchange)

Broadcom: $1.47 trillion

Over the last decade, Nvidia , Apple, Microsoft, and Broadcom have delivered an eye-popping median return of 1,400%. They contributed to a 452% return in the Nasdaq-100 over that period, which was twice the return of the S&P 500.

The Nasdaq-100 also holds large positions in other trillion-dollar giants like Alphabet , Amazon , Tesla , and Meta Platforms . They don't fall into the tech sector specifically, but they are extremely active in emerging industries like artificial intelligence (AI), and their respective stocks have also delivered blistering returns over the last decade.

Story Continues

There are many up-and-coming stocks that could also help drive the Nasdaq-100 higher over the long term:

Advanced Micro Devices , which competes with Nvidia in the market for AI data center chips.

Micron Technology , which supplies data center memory hardware to both Nvidia and AMD

Palantir Technologies , which developed a series of AI-powered software platforms to help organizations extract maximum value from their internal data.

Netflix , which operates the world's largest streaming service for movies and television shows.

CrowdStrike , which developed an AI-powered, all-in-one cybersecurity platform for enterprises.

There is rarely a bad time to invest

Volatility is a normal part of the investing journey. Enduring stock market declines is the price of admission for an opportunity to earn significant returns over the long term. The Nasdaq-100 has experienced five bear markets since the Invesco QQQ ETF was established in 1999, which are defined by peak-to-trough declines of 20% or more.

Each bear market was caused by an entirely different event, like the bursting of the dot-com bubble in the year 2000, the global financial crisis in 2008, the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, the inflation crisis in 2022, and the Trump administration's "Liberation Day" tariffs in 2025. In other words, it's practically impossible to predict when the stock market might slip into bear territory, so staying the course -- even during the most unsettling periods -- is the secret to success.

In fact, the Invesco ETF has still produced a compound annual return of 10.3% since 1999, even after accounting for every sell-off, correction, and bear market along the way. Plus, returns have accelerated to 20.3% per year over the last decade thanks to incredible growth in industries like cloud computing and AI. Therefore, investors who simply stayed in the market over the last 27 years would have done exceptionally well.

AI stocks are likely to continue driving the broader market higher, and with technologies like robotics, autonomous vehicles, and even quantum computing quickly gaining momentum, it's reasonable to expect the Invesco QQQ ETF to trend higher over the long term. As a result, now might be a great time to buy.

news.Title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF During the Stock Market Sell-Off? History Offers a Clear Answer.
news.Content:
Over 3,500 companies have chosen to go public through the Nasdaq stock exchange. The Nasdaq-100 index tracks the performance of the top 100 (by value) companies, excluding banks and other financial institutions.

Since the technology sector is home to more trillion-dollar companies than any other sector, it boasts a dominant weighting of almost 60% in the Nasdaq-100. That means companies at the cutting edge of industries like artificial intelligence (AI) have a significant influence over the performance of the index, which is why it typically delivers higher returns than more diversified indexes like the S&P 500 .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

But that can also be a recipe for volatility. The Nasdaq-100 is currently trading down 8.8% from its all-time high amid rising economic uncertainty and geopolitical tensions, whereas the S&P 500 has declined by a lesser 7%.

The Invesco QQQ Trust (NASDAQ: QQQ) is an exchange-traded fund (ETF) that tracks the performance of the Nasdaq-100 by holding the same stocks and maintaining similar weightings. Is the recent sell-off a buying opportunity for investors? Here's what history says.

Tech stocks tend to lead the market higher

The Nasdaq-100 (and by extension, the Invesco QQQ ETF) invests across 10 different economic sectors, but as I mentioned earlier, almost 60% of the value of its entire portfolio is parked in technology stocks. The tech sector is home to five companies valued at $1 trillion or more, and four of them are among the top holdings in the Nasdaq-100:

Nvidia: $4.2 trillion

Apple: $3.64 trillion

Microsoft: $2.84 trillion

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing: $1.71 trillion (not in the Nasdaq-100, because it's listed on the New York Stock Exchange)

Broadcom: $1.47 trillion

Over the last decade, Nvidia , Apple, Microsoft, and Broadcom have delivered an eye-popping median return of 1,400%. They contributed to a 452% return in the Nasdaq-100 over that period, which was twice the return of the S&P 500.

The Nasdaq-100 also holds large positions in other trillion-dollar giants like Alphabet , Amazon , Tesla , and Meta Platforms . They don't fall into the tech sector specifically, but they are extremely active in emerging industries like artificial intelligence (AI), and their respective stocks have also delivered blistering returns over the last decade.

Story Continues

There are many up-and-coming stocks that could also help drive the Nasdaq-100 higher over the long term:

Advanced Micro Devices , which competes with Nvidia in the market for AI data center chips.

Micron Technology , which supplies data center memory hardware to both Nvidia and AMD

Palantir Technologies , which developed a series of AI-powered software platforms to help organizations extract maximum value from their internal data.

Netflix , which operates the world's largest streaming service for movies and television shows.

CrowdStrike , which developed an AI-powered, all-in-one cybersecurity platform for enterprises.

There is rarely a bad time to invest

Volatility is a normal part of the investing journey. Enduring stock market declines is the price of admission for an opportunity to earn significant returns over the long term. The Nasdaq-100 has experienced five bear markets since the Invesco QQQ ETF was established in 1999, which are defined by peak-to-trough declines of 20% or more.

Each bear market was caused by an entirely different event, like the bursting of the dot-com bubble in the year 2000, the global financial crisis in 2008, the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, the inflation crisis in 2022, and the Trump administration's "Liberation Day" tariffs in 2025. In other words, it's practically impossible to predict when the stock market might slip into bear territory, so staying the course -- even during the most unsettling periods -- is the secret to success.

In fact, the Invesco ETF has still produced a compound annual return of 10.3% since 1999, even after accounting for every sell-off, correction, and bear market along the way. Plus, returns have accelerated to 20.3% per year over the last decade thanks to incredible growth in industries like cloud computing and AI. Therefore, investors who simply stayed in the market over the last 27 years would have done exceptionally well.

AI stocks are likely to continue driving the broader market higher, and with technologies like robotics, autonomous vehicles, and even quantum computing quickly gaining momentum, it's reasonable to expect the Invesco QQQ ETF to trend higher over the long term. As a result, now might be a great time to buy.

news.Title: 三大法人再砍1046億！台股爆跌821點「投信還在撐」　散戶悲嘆：被賣爛了
news.Content:
美伊局勢未有緩解跡象，台股今（23日）開盤再爆跌超過千點，終場跌幅稍稍收斂，仍下跌821點，收32,722點；台積電驚險守住1800元大關，下跌30元，收1810元。根據證交所公布籌碼動向，外資連3賣，再狠砍839億元，投信逆勢進貨104億元，三大法人合計賣超1046億元。繼上週四之後，今日再現賣超千億元，股民對市場開始轉趨悲觀，直呼「整個被賣爛」、「洗碗悲歌」。

台股延續上週跌勢，今日開盤再度重挫將近1100點，不僅失守3萬3千點大關，最低觸及32,461.09點；台積電（2330）跌破1800元，來到1785元；鴻海（2317）也跌破200元；記憶體族群多頭氣勢也面臨挑戰，多檔跌停鎖死。不過，盤中台積電買盤進駐，大盤跌幅收斂。集中市場最後下跌821.38點，收32,722.50點；台積電下跌30元，收1810元；鴻海持續疲弱，下跌7元，收196元。

盤後證交所公布籌碼動向，外資及陸資（不含外資自營商）連3賣，賣超839.22億元；自營商（合計）賣超311.1億元，其中自營商自行買賣方面，賣超60.24億元；避險部分，賣超250.86億元；投信則買超104.07億元，三大法人今日共賣超1046.27億元。

由於台股繼續重挫，三大法人近3個交易日，已兩天出現賣超千億的數字，股民見外資拚命倒貨，情緒開始轉趨悲觀，也擔心投信快要扛不住了，在社群平台哀號，「源源不絕的提款，台股沒救」、「整個被賣爛」、「大媽不怕死」、「投信接到手斷掉」、「大媽還能接嗎」、「少年股神明天開始要跑外送了」。
news.AI_Summary:
• 受美伊局勢影響，台股23日開盤重挫逾千點，終場下跌821點，收32,722點。
• 台積電（2330）驚險守住1800元關卡，下跌30元，收1810元。
• 外資連3賣，再砍839億元，投信逆勢進貨104億元，三大法人合計賣超1046億元。
• 三大法人近3個交易日已兩天出現賣超千億元，股民情緒轉趨悲觀。
• 記憶體族群多檔跌停鎖死，PCB、低軌衛星等也成殺盤重心。
news.Keywords: 地緣政治風險, 外資賣超, 投信買超, 權值股, 恐慌性賣壓

news.Title: AI Doomsday: Why 1 Crashing Crypto Could Be a Once-in-a-Decade Buying Opportunity
news.Content:
On Feb. 22, Citrini Research dropped a piece titled "The 2028 Global Intelligence Crisis" that rattled investor confidence in the market and economy. It highlights the possible negative results if artificial intelligence (AI) rapidly progresses.

Just days after the report, fintech powerhouse Block added to the worries when it announced layoffs of 40% to its staff.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Since the research memo was released, the S&P 500 index is down more than 4% (as of March 19). Financial stocks tied to spending activity, like American Express and Capital One , are off by double digits.

The turmoil and uncertainty point to this crashing cryptocurrency being a once-in-a-decade buying opportunity.

Counting on the government to step in and provide support

The main takeaway from the Citrini report is that AI productivity enhancements will lead to a wave of corporate layoffs, particularly for white-collar workers. This will result in a drastic reduction to incomes earned and spending power, which will reduce demand across the economy. In this scenario, unemployment soars and wages tank.

Despite how scary this sounds, people can count on the government to step in and provide support. This would actually introduce a powerful tailwind for a scarce asset like Bitcoin , which is 44% off its peak. Cutting interest rates, and keeping them near zero, and printing money are the tools. Plus, a wave of regulations could be introduced to protect workers in various industries.

Providing support is exactly what the U.S. did on two separate occasions in the past couple of decades. During the time of the Great Recession, the government injected trillions of dollars of stimulus into the economy through different programs. Then, during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020, the government stepped in again, only this time with more firepower and at a faster pace.

Bitcoin, which was created and launched on the heels of the Great Recession, is up 9,000,000% in 15 years. It has been a direct beneficiary of the government's reckless fiscal and monetary policies.

Ignoring these fears, this asset still has a promising future

Any headlines hinting at possible AI-induced doomsday scenarios get a lot of attention these days. I don't believe there's a reason to worry, though.

Integrating AI throughout the economy will take time. It's not going to happen overnight. Plus, not everything can or will be automated. What's more, new jobs will be created, as has been the case throughout history.

Story Continues

These AI fears are overblown, in my opinion. But Bitcoin still has significant long-term upside, based on the same argument about fiscal and monetary policies providing a persistent liquidity boom. Even when the economy isn't facing a severe recession or generational health crisis, sovereign debt and money supply levels keep rising.

That alarming trend is not going to end. And it supports the case for Bitcoin being a once-in-a-decade buying opportunity right now.

news.Title: Palantir's Stock Valuation Still Looks Absurd. Here's Why Investors Keep Buying It Anyway.
news.Content:
When OpenAI publicly released ChatGPT on Nov. 30, 2022, shares of Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ: PLTR) were trading for $6. Today, the stock hovers around $150 -- a nearly 2,000% gain in just three years.

With a market cap of $368 billion, Palantir is almost worth the combined value of legacy enterprise software stocks Salesforce and SAP . It's obvious by now that demand for artificial intelligence (AI) is the core tailwind fueling Palantir's parabolic rise.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The question some are asking is whether Palantir's soaring valuation is sustainable. Below, I'll break down why smart investors continue to buy the stock hand over fist -- despite the appearance of an overvalued name benefiting from AI-driven tailwinds.

Why does Palantir stock look so expensive?

When I was an investment banking analyst, I spent countless hours putting together comparable company analyses. In layman's terms, this means that I benchmarked our client against a peer set of comparable businesses in the same industry to derive a valuation range.

Broadly speaking, analysts will look at price-to-sales (P/S), price-to-earnings (P/E), or enterprise value-to-EBITDA multiples depending on the company and industry in question. As far as software stocks are concerned, the P/S ratio is a good metric to start with. Many SaaS businesses are not consistently profitable, and so measuring a cohort on a revenue basis levels the playing field.

If you were to base an investment in Palantir purely off of the figures above, you'd come to the quick conclusion that Palantir stock is absurdly overvalued relative to other leading SaaS enterprises. While I understand that logic, it's flawed.

A once-in-a-generation opportunity: Palantir is actually dirt cheap

Smart investors understand that peer analysis has its shortcomings. While companies like Snowflake , ServiceNow , Databricks, and MongoDB each represent high-growth players in their respective software verticals, none are true, direct competitors to Palantir.

This raises the question: Why did I include these companies in my analysis at all? Well, I more or less had to. You see, data analytics, database management, and cybersecurity are relatively commoditized businesses.

While Palantir is often touted for its data-driven actionable insights, the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) is far more complex. With its Foundry and Gotham suites, Palantir not only aggregates and synthesizes data, but allows decision-makers to simulate critical workflows in real time.

Story Continues

To get a sense of how important Palantir's services are, consider the company is currently working alongside the U.S. military in the Iran war . Moreover, the company has a $10 billion contract with the Army as well as a total backlog that's growing over 100% annually across accelerating commercial and public sector adoption.

Bulls might say that the software industry hasn't seen a company with Palantir's potential since Microsoft in the mid 1980s. Just as Microsoft built a near-monopoly in personal computing, Palantir could be on its way to achieving a similar scale of domination across enterprise software workflows.

With annual revenue growth near 60%, consistent profitability, and its services spanning every major sector, I suspect Palantir has extremely low churn rates as its customers find AIP indispensable relative to other products in the market.

For this reason, some investors view Palantir stock as dirt cheap. Hence, they continue to pound the buy button.

news.Title: 玉山投信稱，能源供應中斷風險升溫，上週台韓印成賣超重心
news.Content:
【財訊快報／記者劉居全報導】全球能源供應中斷風險升高，市場資金也開始提高戒備，玉山投信指出，上週國際資金持續自亞股撤出，除了東北亞雙雄台灣與南韓分別遭賣超56.46億美元、22.08億美元外，印度也遭提款26.67億美元，成為亞股中最悲情的三大市場。不過從亞股表現來看漲跌互見，反映出內資態度並非與外資同步，玉山投信表示，上週韓股大舉反彈5.36%，完全無懼外資賣超影響，泰國、馬來西亞單週也有逾1%漲幅，但相對地，陸股、越南股市分別下跌2.19%、2.86%。

保德信全球藍籌ETF(009810)經理人黃相慈表示，上週布蘭特原油一度衝破112美元，加大通膨反彈的壓力，市場也擔心將影響聯準會降息節奏，甚至部分交易員認為今年可能轉為升息，美債殖利率隨之走高，進一步壓抑美股走勢，短期地緣政治風險升高、油價高檔震盪與利率政策不確定性的影響之下，預估短期市場波動恐加劇。不過，過去5次重要戰爭事件發生後半年與一年，美股平均漲幅約12%，顯示地緣政治衝擊多屬短線干擾，中長期趨勢仍回歸基本面與經濟成長動能。

黃相慈指出，此次FOMC會議雖上調今年核心PCE預估至2.7%，然而GDP也上修至2.4%，顯示聯準會官員對經濟動能的信心有所提升。至於AI趨勢指標之一的輝達GTC大會中，執行長黃仁勳表示AI晶片到明年的潛在商機將達1兆美元，整體來看，在美國經濟維持成長，同時AI商機持續擴張的情況下，美股仍具備基本面與獲利成長雙重支撐。

針對美股走勢，黃相慈分析，就技術面觀察，美股今年以來回檔5%，整體仍處於高檔修正階段，短期內指數走勢預期將維持震盪整理格局。目前下方支撐區有機會進一步測試位於6449點的年線位置，若指數能於年線獲得支撐並止穩，則有助於修復市場信心，並為後續反彈行情奠定基礎。

至於歐洲方面，黃相慈說明，歐洲央行(ECB)、英國央行(BoE)、瑞士央行及瑞典央行於上週均決議維持基準利率不變，不過，ECB會後聲明透露出偏鷹的政策訊號，受到中東戰事升溫推動能源價格大幅上行的背景下，歐元區正面臨通膨上行風險與經濟成長放緩壓力，若情勢進一步惡化，將準備採取必要行動，以確保通膨回到2%的目標。

黃相慈指出，ECB總裁拉加德表示，央行目前「處於良好位置，且具備充分政策工具」，足以因應潛在的重大外部衝擊，此番言論帶動利率預期快速調整，利率期貨市場報價顯示，市場預期今年ECB可能升息2碼以因應通膨風險，而市場也預估ECB最快將於4月開始討論升息，並且於6月開始實施。

陸股方面，PGIM保德信中國中小基金經理人陳舜津指出，受中東地緣衝突升溫影響，川普原定於兩週後出訪北京的行程延後約一個半月，市場目前預期將於5月中旬訪問中國，在短期缺乏正面催化劑的情況下，A股市場亦受到一定衝擊，指數呈現震盪走勢。

不過，陳舜津表示，從資金面來看，近期滬深兩市成交金額仍穩定維持在2兆人民幣以上，顯示市場買盤尚未明顯退潮，加上強勢人民幣匯率有望提升人民幣資產吸引力，外資主動型資金入市意願提升，為市場籌碼增加挹注。惟需留意，市場結構性分歧仍將持續，產業表現呈現輪動格局，其中以AI為核心的科技板塊，仍是企業獲利成長的主要驅動力，在基本面支撐下，由成長股主導的市場風格可望延續。

受到上週五美股大跌影響，今(23)日台股大跌2.45%，下挫821.38點，指數收在32722.5點，成交金額6770.28億元。保德信市值動能50 ETF(009803)經理人楊立楷指出，央行上修2026年經濟成長率至7.28%，搭配台灣外銷訂單前2月累計創新高，反映出台灣經濟基本面仍相當有力，科技業訂單及獲利維持強勁。然而受到美伊戰事持續緊繃，甚至有愈演愈烈的態勢，加上美歐台等央行的利率決策出現鷹派言論，預期短線將以整理方式消化賣壓，布局台股宜低接不追高。

楊立楷表示，雖然短期台股受到地緣政治事件左右，不過從上週全球科技業矚目的輝達GTC大會可窺知，AI已正式進入「大量實際使用」階段，重點不再只是訓練模型，而是讓AI持續進行思考、推理與執行任務，為未來資料中心的角色將從傳統資料儲存設施，轉變為生產AI推理代幣的智慧工廠，2027年整體AI基礎建設需求將達1兆美元以上。

楊立楷說明，整體來看，本次大會傳遞出AI應用加速落地的正向訊號，並有利於帶動AI伺服器、散熱、網通與相關零組件的長期需求成長。目前Vera Rubin平台已獲AWS、Google Cloud、微軟Azure、Oracle Cloud等雲端業者支持，預計下半年開始陸續上市，將挹注台廠相關供應鏈需求持續成長。
news.AI_Summary:
• 玉山投信指出，全球能源供應中斷風險升高，上週國際資金持續自亞股撤出，台灣、南韓、印度遭賣超最多。
• 上週韓股大漲5.36%，泰國、馬來西亞也有逾1%漲幅，但陸股、越南股市下跌。
• 布蘭特原油一度衝破112美元，加大通膨反彈壓力，影響聯準會降息節奏。
• 美股短期受地緣政治風險、油價高檔及利率政策不確定性影響，預估短期波動恐加劇。
• 儘管短期有波動，但中長期趨勢仍回歸基本面與經濟成長動能，AI商機持續擴張。
news.Keywords: 資金流向, 亞股, 原油價格, 通膨疑慮, AI商機

news.Title: Prediction: This Will Be Micron's Stock Price by Late 2027
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence accelerators developed by companies like Nvidia and Broadcom require more memory than traditional processors. That has made memory chip manufacturer Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) one of the hottest stocks on the market.

Demand for memory chips has led to an unprecedented supply shortage, causing prices to skyrocket. Indeed, dynamic random access memory (DRAM) prices have nearly tripled over the past year, according to The Wall Street Journal . And Micron stock has advanced 350% to $423 per share over the same period.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Here's my prediction: Micron will trade around $554 per share after the company delivers its fourth-quarter financial report in late 2027. That implies 31% upside from its current share price.

Micron reported exceptional financial results in the second quarter

Micron is a semiconductor company that develops memory and storage solutions for personal computers, mobile devices, data center servers, and automotive systems. It is the third-largest manufacturer of DRAM memory products, including the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and NAND flash memory products behind South Korean companies Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix .

Micron reported exceptional financial results in the second quarter of fiscal 2026 (ended Feb. 26). Revenue increased 196% to $23.8 billion, driven by record sales in DRAM, HBM, and NAND memory products. Non-GAAP net income increased 682% to $12.20 per diluted share. But the stock declined following the report because investors are uncertain how long the good times will last.

The memory chip industry is defined by boom-and-bust cycles

Memory chips are commodities in that little differentiation exists between products from different suppliers. That means memory chip manufacturers compete primarily on price, which itself is determined by supply and demand.

Historically, the memory chip industry has oscillated between supply shortages that drive prices higher and supply gluts that drive prices lower. That happens because suppliers like Micron increase production capacity during inflection points that boost demand. That is an expensive and time-consuming endeavor, but supply eventually outpaces demand, which explains the cyclical nature of the industry.

The last inflection point was the COVID-19 pandemic. Demand for memory chips started to increase in mid-2020 as businesses shifted toward remote work, creating a need for more personal computing devices and data center capacity. Memory chip prices peaked in 2022 before falling significantly in 2023, at which point suppliers cut production capacity to keep prices above manufacturing costs.

Story Continues

In the aftermath, memory and storage companies hesitated increase production capacity as the artificial intelligence (AI) boom took shape, according to Thomas Coughlin, a technology consultant who specializes in the data storage industry. "There was little or no investment in new production capacity in 2024 and through most 2025." That was the root cause of the current supply shortage.

Wall Street expects Micron's earnings to peak in 2027

Currently, memory chip suppliers Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix are racing to increase production capacity, and several new wafer fabrication facilities and packaging plants are expected to be operational by 2027. It may take time for supply to match demand, but the market may start looking through demand at that point to the supply glut that is sure to follow.

Micron stock currently trades at 19 times adjusted times earnings, a cheap valuation for a company whose adjusted earnings increased more than 600% last quarter. But I think the market will afford Micron a much cheaper multiple once additional memory chip production capacity comes online. This is where my anecdote about the post-pandemic supply glut is relevant.

Micron reported solid financial results in fiscal 2022. Revenue increased 11% to a record $30.8 billion, and non-GAAP net income soared 38% to $8.35 per diluted share. Yet, shares traded at just 6 times adjusted earnings after the company's report because the market was anticipating the post-pandemic supply glut.

This time around, Wall Street still expects Micron's adjusted earnings to peak at $92.35 per diluted share in fiscal 2027 before declining 78% to $20.57 per diluted share in 2029. If the market affords Micron the same valuation multiple that it did when the last memory chip cycle peaked (i.e., 6 times adjusted earnings), the stock will trade at $554 per share in late 2027, assuming Wall Street's forward earnings forecast is correct.

news.Title: Prediction: This Will Be Micron's Stock Price by Late 2027
news.Content:
Artificial intelligence accelerators developed by companies like Nvidia and Broadcom require more memory than traditional processors. That has made memory chip manufacturer Micron Technology (NASDAQ: MU) one of the hottest stocks on the market.

Demand for memory chips has led to an unprecedented supply shortage, causing prices to skyrocket. Indeed, dynamic random access memory (DRAM) prices have nearly tripled over the past year, according to The Wall Street Journal . And Micron stock has advanced 350% to $423 per share over the same period.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Here's my prediction: Micron will trade around $554 per share after the company delivers its fourth-quarter financial report in late 2027. That implies 31% upside from its current share price.

Micron reported exceptional financial results in the second quarter

Micron is a semiconductor company that develops memory and storage solutions for personal computers, mobile devices, data center servers, and automotive systems. It is the third-largest manufacturer of DRAM memory products, including the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) and NAND flash memory products behind South Korean companies Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix .

Micron reported exceptional financial results in the second quarter of fiscal 2026 (ended Feb. 26). Revenue increased 196% to $23.8 billion, driven by record sales in DRAM, HBM, and NAND memory products. Non-GAAP net income increased 682% to $12.20 per diluted share. But the stock declined following the report because investors are uncertain how long the good times will last.

The memory chip industry is defined by boom-and-bust cycles

Memory chips are commodities in that little differentiation exists between products from different suppliers. That means memory chip manufacturers compete primarily on price, which itself is determined by supply and demand.

Historically, the memory chip industry has oscillated between supply shortages that drive prices higher and supply gluts that drive prices lower. That happens because suppliers like Micron increase production capacity during inflection points that boost demand. That is an expensive and time-consuming endeavor, but supply eventually outpaces demand, which explains the cyclical nature of the industry.

The last inflection point was the COVID-19 pandemic. Demand for memory chips started to increase in mid-2020 as businesses shifted toward remote work, creating a need for more personal computing devices and data center capacity. Memory chip prices peaked in 2022 before falling significantly in 2023, at which point suppliers cut production capacity to keep prices above manufacturing costs.

Story Continues

In the aftermath, memory and storage companies hesitated increase production capacity as the artificial intelligence (AI) boom took shape, according to Thomas Coughlin, a technology consultant who specializes in the data storage industry. "There was little or no investment in new production capacity in 2024 and through most 2025." That was the root cause of the current supply shortage.

Wall Street expects Micron's earnings to peak in 2027

Currently, memory chip suppliers Micron, Samsung, and SK Hynix are racing to increase production capacity, and several new wafer fabrication facilities and packaging plants are expected to be operational by 2027. It may take time for supply to match demand, but the market may start looking through demand at that point to the supply glut that is sure to follow.

Micron stock currently trades at 19 times adjusted times earnings, a cheap valuation for a company whose adjusted earnings increased more than 600% last quarter. But I think the market will afford Micron a much cheaper multiple once additional memory chip production capacity comes online. This is where my anecdote about the post-pandemic supply glut is relevant.

Micron reported solid financial results in fiscal 2022. Revenue increased 11% to a record $30.8 billion, and non-GAAP net income soared 38% to $8.35 per diluted share. Yet, shares traded at just 6 times adjusted earnings after the company's report because the market was anticipating the post-pandemic supply glut.

This time around, Wall Street still expects Micron's adjusted earnings to peak at $92.35 per diluted share in fiscal 2027 before declining 78% to $20.57 per diluted share in 2029. If the market affords Micron the same valuation multiple that it did when the last memory chip cycle peaked (i.e., 6 times adjusted earnings), the stock will trade at $554 per share in late 2027, assuming Wall Street's forward earnings forecast is correct.

news.Title: 《日股》日經大跌1857點 今年漲幅幾近歸零
news.Content:
【時報編譯柳繼剛綜合外電報導】中東戰事迄今沒有趨緩下，外界憂心通膨再起以及經濟成長受到衝擊，日經23日一開盤就跌破五萬三整數關卡，盤中賣壓不斷最後甚至失守五萬二，收51,515.49點，大跌1,857.04點或3.48%，今年迄今漲幅大幅縮水至2%左右。同時，東證收在3,486.44點，也大跌122.96點或3.41%。

路透社指出，日經早盤一度重挫超過5%，曾把今年以來的所有漲幅都吃掉。二月時，日經指數曾飆升至59,332.43點歷史新高，並有望突破60,000點大關，主要受惠於日相高市早苗的刺激計畫，可望加速日企成長。

因日本石油供應的關鍵水道荷姆茲海峽仍然被封鎖，故日經指數23日交易時的表現跟歷史高點相比，已大幅下挫超過14%，主要是因為，日本約有90%的石油運輸都依賴荷姆茲海峽。

分析師指出，在中東衝突持續沒有停歇之下，油價短時間內大漲，迫使日企可能不得不下調下一個財年的業績預估。另外，國際油價大漲也會拖累到經濟成長，進而影響到企業的獲利能力。

日本央行上周維持利率不變，但警告油價飆升可能會引起通膨大漲，因此保留提前升息的可能性。分析師表示，通膨可能會對全球央行帶來升息的壓力，對股市不利外，對債市也不好。日本10年期公債殖利率，一度上漲六個基本點，升至2.320%，創一月21日以來最高。

主要晶片類股大都走低，例如，愛德萬測試大跌5.2%，東京威力科創大跌2.6%。人工智慧AI概念股中，軟銀跌1.4%，藤倉大跌4%，古河電氣工業大跌近6%，住友電氣工業也重挫超過7%。
news.AI_Summary:
• 受中東戰事影響，日經23日大跌1857點（3.48%），收51,515.49點，今年漲幅大幅縮水至2%左右。
• 東證收盤也下跌3.41%。
• 日經指數較歷史高點已大幅下挫超過14%，因日本約90%石油運輸依賴荷姆茲海峽。
• 分析師指出，油價大漲可能迫使日企下調業績預估，拖累經濟成長與企業獲利。
• 日本央行警告油價飆升可能引起通膨大漲，保留提前升息可能性。
news.Keywords: 股市重挫, 地緣政治風險, 油價上漲, 通膨, 升息預期

news.Title: The Projected Federal Reserve Script Has Been Flipped -- and the Stock Market Isn't Ready for It
news.Content:
For years, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJINDICES: ^DJI) , S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) , and Nasdaq Composite (NASDAQINDEX: ^IXIC) have appeared unstoppable. The evolution of artificial intelligence (AI), record corporate share buybacks, and the prospect of lower interest rates have fueled this bull market rally.

But the foundation for one of Wall Street's key pillars is crumbling, and neither Wall Street nor everyday investors are ready for it.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Say goodbye to the Federal Reserve's rate-easing cycle?

With the stock market entering 2026 at its second-priciest valuation since 1871 , according to the S&P 500's Shiller Price-to-Earnings (P/E) Ratio, investors have been counting on additional interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve to fuel growth. Reducing lending rates can encourage businesses to borrow, ultimately leading to increases in hiring, innovation, and acquisitions. Lowering interest rates is similar to pressing the accelerator in a car to make it go faster.

But the Iran war has thrown a monkey wrench into the central bank's plans.

Since the U.S. and Israel launched military attacks against Iran on Feb. 28, oil prices have soared. This is in direct response to Iran's virtual closure of the Strait of Hormuz, through which approximately 20% of the world's liquid petroleum travels daily. This oil price shock is expected to resonate throughout the U.S. economy and notably raise the inflation rate in the months to come.

According to estimates from the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta, courtesy of Carson Group's Chief Market Strategist, Ryan Detrick, there's now a higher probability of the Fed hiking rates over the next three months than there is of the central bank cutting rates.

Just as lowering borrowing costs is designed to accelerate economic growth, raising interest rates often cools lending demand and tempers growth expectations. It wouldn't be particularly good news for the AI-driven technology sector.

Story Continues

If the Fed's script flips, Wall Street's bull market rally may be over

While higher interest rates can make borrowing costlier for consumers and businesses, they can also be devastating for the stock market.

The S&P 500's Shiller P/E, also known as the Cyclically Adjusted P/E Ratio (CAPE Ratio), has averaged 17.35 over the last 155 years. Over the last five months, it's been vacillating between 39 and 41. History has made clear that extended valuation premiums aren't sustainable over the long term.

The CAPE Ratio has exceeded 30 on six occasions since 1871, including the present. Following the five previous instances, the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and/or Nasdaq Composite fell by at least 20% each time. After the Shiller P/E peaked at 44.19 in December 1999, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq shed 49% and 78% of their respective value.

If the Fed's script flips from rate-easing to rate-hiking, arguably, the bedrock of this historically expensive stock market will be fractured beyond repair. Even removing the possibility of rate cuts from the table may be enough to take the wind out of Wall Street's sails.

While more than a century of data shows that stocks offer the highest long-term annualized returns of all asset classes, the table appears set for a bumpy ride on Wall Street over the next few quarters.

news.Title: 川普「1句話」台股狂瀉821點！台積電穩守成交王　這檔緊咬輝達商機成交額衝180億元
news.Content:
[ FTNN新聞網 ]記者邱梓欣／綜合報導

美國總統川普日前脫口「不想停火」，引發市場避險情緒升溫，美股於上週五全面收黑，影響台股今（23）日早盤便下挫逾千點，終場收盤以32,722.50作收，下跌821.38點，跌幅2.45％，成交金額6770.28億元。觀察今日盤後成交額排行個股，晶圓代工龍頭台積電（2330）今早盤一度失守1800元大關，甚至最低探到1785元、下跌55元；終場跌幅收斂收在1810元，下跌30元，跌幅1.63％，成交金額則破626億元，穩居盤後成交王。

僅次於台積電的台達電，由於輝達伺服器從GB300升級至Vera Rubin，因此把電壓提高到800伏特。為此，台達電憑藉研發多年的「固態變壓器」技術，成功將電網3萬伏特的交流電，一次轉為800伏特的直流電，成功成為輝達重要夥伴之一；產品也在日前輝達GTC大會上，被擺在專區第一位。

根據《yahoo!股市》資料顯示，盤後個股成交額排行前五名依序為：第一名、台積電（2330）成交額626.10億元。第二名、台達電（2308）成交額180.80億元。第三名、南亞科（2408）成交額171.23億元。第四名、力積電（6770）成交額170.05億元。第五名、華邦電（2344）成交額155.65億元。

◎《FTNN新聞網》提醒您：本資料僅供參考，投資人應獨立判斷，審慎評估並自負投資風險。
news.AI_Summary:
• 美國總統川普關於「不想停火」的言論引發市場避險情緒升溫，美股上週五全面收黑，影響台股23日早盤下挫逾千點。
• 台股終場下跌821.38點，收32,722.50點，成交金額6770.28億元。
• 台積電（2330）盤中一度失守1800元，終場收1810元，下跌30元，成交金額破626億元，穩居盤後成交王。
• 台達電（2308）因輝達伺服器升級至Vera Rubin，受惠於固態變壓器技術，成為輝達重要夥伴，盤後成交額居第二。
• 盤後個股成交額排行前五名依序為：台積電、台達電、南亞科、力積電、華邦電。
news.Keywords: 地緣政治風險, 避險情緒, 權值股, AI伺服器, 成交量

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人23日買超前十大 偏愛電子 掃貨機票連6買
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股23日加權指數收32722.50點，跌821.38點或2.45%，總成交值7114.80億元。三大法人賣超1046.27億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，投信、自營商分別買超104.07億元及賣超311.12億元。

三大法人買超前十大個股，依類別分，6檔電子股、1檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，3檔百元股、5檔中價位股及2檔銅板股；買超張數方面，2檔買超上萬張，榜首為長榮航(2618)，買超2萬4965張，大聯大(3702)、中信金(2891)名列二、三名，分別買超1萬389張及買超9193張。第四及第五名為群創(3481)、華邦電(2344)，其他依序為康霈*(6919)、東和鋼鐵(2006)、台灣大(3045)、中華電(2412)及兆赫(2485)。

至於前十大個股中買超金額前三大，則為大聯大(3702)9.42億元、長榮航(2618)8.70億元、華邦電(2344)6.20億元。

●三大法人23日買超前十名：

1.長榮航(2618)收34.85元，漲0.05元或0.14%，成交量4.64萬張，三大法人買超2萬4965張、估8.70億元，為連6日買超，累計買超6萬8171張、估23.82億元。

2.大聯大(3702)收90.70元，跌0.70元或0.77%，成交量1.86萬張，三大法人買超1萬389張、估9.42億元，為連8日買超，累計買超6萬4117張、估57.86億元。

3.中信金(2891)收51.50元，跌0.60元或1.15%，成交量6.11萬張，三大法人買超9193張、估4.73億元，由連3日累計賣超1萬3788張轉為買超。

4.群創(3481)收25.00元，跌1.25元或4.76%，成交量26.94萬張，三大法人買超6894張、估1.72億元，由連6日累計賣超44萬2268張轉為買超。

5.華邦電(2344)收107.50元，跌2.50元或2.27%，成交量14.87萬張，三大法人買超5765張、估6.20億元，由連2日累計賣超10萬9139張轉為買超。

6.康霈*(6919)收94.50元，跌7.00元或6.90%，成交量1.47萬張，三大法人買超5194張、估4.91億元，由前一交易日賣超1319張轉為買超。

7.東和鋼鐵(2006)收71.20元，漲0.70元或0.99%，成交量0.69萬張，三大法人買超4764張、估3.39億元，為連2日買超，累計買超1萬2899張、估9.12億元。

8.台灣大(3045)收109.00元，股價持平，成交量0.81萬張，三大法人買超4231張、估4.61億元，為連6日買超，累計買超1萬9988張、估21.85億元。

9.中華電(2412)收134.00元，股價持平，成交量1.66萬張，三大法人買超3913張、估5.24億元，為連21日買超，累計買超8萬3489張、估113.77億元。

10.兆赫(2485)收78.00元，漲4.10元或5.55%，成交量1.52萬張，三大法人買超3595張、估2.80億元，為連2日買超，累計買超5131張、估3.93億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.長榮航(2618)三大法人累計買超4萬3206張，其中外資買超3萬4956張、投信買超6774張、自營商買超1476張；融資增54張、融券減40張，券資比0.44%。

2.大聯大(3702)三大法人累計買超5萬2961張，其中外資買超5萬4695張、投信買超115張、自營商賣超1850張；融資增394張、融券增220張，券資比9.34%。

3.中信金(2891)三大法人累計賣超1萬1659張，其中外資賣超2萬4365張、投信買超1萬181張、自營商買超2524張；融資減439張、融券增5張，券資比6.65%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅23日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。(編輯：邱致馨)
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股23日下跌821.38點，收32722.50點，三大法人合計賣超1046.27億元。
• 外資賣超839.23億元，投信買超104.07億元，自營商賣超311.12億元。
• 三大法人買超前十大個股中，長榮航（2618）買超張數最多，其次為大聯大（3702）、中信金（2891）。
• 買超金額前三大為大聯大（3702）、長榮航（2618）、華邦電（2344）。
• 投信已連續買超長榮航6日、大聯大8日、台灣大6日、中華電21日。
news.Keywords: 三大法人, 買超股, 籌碼流向, 航運股, 電子股

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人23日賣超前十大 龍頭倒一地 連砍力積電、友達
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股23日加權指數收32722.50點，跌821.38點或2.45%，總成交值7114.80億元。三大法人賣超1046.27億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，投信、自營商分別買超104.07億元及賣超311.12億元。

三大法人賣超前十大個股，依類別分，3檔電子股、3檔金融股及4檔傳產股；依股價分，1檔百元股、2檔中價位股及7檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，10檔賣超上萬張，榜首為中鋼(2002)，賣超4萬8004張，力積電(6770)、友達(2409)名列二、三名，分別賣超3萬9992張及賣超3萬6131張。第四及第五名為鴻海(2317)、中石化(1314)，其他依序為台塑(1301)、台新新光金(2887)、台玻(1802)、合庫金(5880)及臺企銀(2834)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為鴻海(2317)55.58億元、力積電(6770)27.63億元、中鋼(2002)9.14億元。

●三大法人23日賣超前十名：

1.中鋼(2002)收19.05元，跌0.40元或2.06%，成交量7.98萬張，三大法人賣超4萬8004張、估9.14億元，由前一交易日買超2萬9585張轉為賣超。

2.力積電(6770)收69.10元，跌6.40元或8.48%，成交量24.35萬張，三大法人賣超3萬9992張、估27.63億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超5萬3041張、估37.48億元。

3.友達(2409)收14.85元，跌0.75元或4.81%，成交量8.81萬張，三大法人賣超3萬6131張、估5.37億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超8萬6299張、估13.32億元。

4.鴻海(2317)收196.00元，跌7.00元或3.45%，成交量7.85萬張，三大法人賣超2萬8359張、估55.58億元，為連8日賣超，累計賣超19萬4901張、估405.3億元。

5.中石化(1314)收8.40元，跌0.09元或1.06%，成交量10.53萬張，三大法人賣超2萬6376張、估2.22億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超3萬2045張、估2.7億元。

6.台塑(1301)收44.75元，跌2.85元或5.99%，成交量4.76萬張，三大法人賣超1萬9720張、估8.82億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超3萬1576張、估14.48億元。

7.台新新光金(2887)收23.90元，跌0.75元或3.04%，成交量13.10萬張，三大法人賣超1萬9089張、估4.56億元，由連2日累計買超3萬169張轉為賣超。

8.台玻(1802)收51.90元，跌5.70元或9.90%，成交量8.32萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6674張、估8.65億元，為連8日賣超，累計賣超10萬2321張、估61.19億元。

9.合庫金(5880)收22.55元，跌0.40元或1.74%，成交量2.45萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6476張、估3.72億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超3萬1115張、估7.08億元。

10.臺企銀(2834)收14.85元，跌0.15元或1.00%，成交量3.08萬張，三大法人賣超1萬6449張、估2.44億元，為連16日賣超，累計賣超26萬3714張、估40.1億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.中鋼(2002)三大法人累計賣超6萬6496張，其中外資賣超6萬6670張、投信賣超1300張、自營商買超1474張；融資增2327張、融券增62張，券資比0.26%。

2.力積電(6770)三大法人累計買超2萬9312張，其中外資買超2萬1808張、投信賣超582張、自營商買超8086張；融資增2萬361張、融券增2958張，券資比5.02%。

3.友達(2409)三大法人累計賣超13萬316張，其中外資賣超12萬8320張、投信賣超8199張、自營商買超6203張；融資增3615張、融券減1萬2890張，券資比0.00%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅23日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。(編輯：龍彩霖)

news.Title: 《台北股市》三大法人23日買超前十大 偏愛電子 掃貨機票連6買
news.Content:

三大法人買超前十大個股，依類別分，6檔電子股、1檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，3檔百元股、5檔中價位股及2檔銅板股；買超張數方面，2檔買超上萬張，榜首為長榮航(2618)，買超2萬4965張，大聯大(3702)、中信金(2891)名列二、三名，分別買超1萬389張及買超9193張。第四及第五名為群創(3481)、華邦電(2344)，其他依序為康霈*(6919)、東和鋼鐵(2006)、台灣大(3045)、中華電(2412)及兆赫(2485)。

news.Title: 《台北股市》外資23日賣超前十大 海公公出大事 金「鋼」慘破10萬張
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】台股23日加權指數收32722.50點，跌821.38點或2.45%，總成交值7114.80億元。三大法人賣超1046.27億元，其中外資賣超839.23億元，投信、自營商分別買超104.07億元及賣超311.12億元。

外資賣超前十大個股，依類別分，3檔電子股、4檔金融股及3檔傳產股；依股價分，1檔百元股、1檔中價位股及8檔銅板股；賣超張數方面，10檔賣超上萬張，榜首為台新新光金(2887)，賣超7萬2426張，中鋼(2002)、力積電(6770)名列二、三名，分別賣超4萬6864張及賣超3萬4329張。第四及第五名為友達(2409)、鴻海(2317)，其他依序為中石化(1314)、台塑(1301)、永豐金(2890)、合庫金(5880)及臺企銀(2834)。

至於前十大個股中賣超金額前三大，則為鴻海(2317)55.71億元、力積電(6770)23.72億元、台新新光金(2887)17.31億元。

●外資23日賣超前十名：

1.台新新光金(2887)收23.90元，跌0.75元或3.04%，成交量13.10萬張，外資賣超7萬2426張、估17.31億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超7萬7003張、估18.44億元。

2.中鋼(2002)收19.05元，跌0.40元或2.06%，成交量7.98萬張，外資賣超4萬6864張、估8.93億元，由前一交易日買超3萬3587張轉為賣超。

3.力積電(6770)收69.10元，跌6.40元或8.48%，成交量24.35萬張，外資賣超3萬4329張、估23.72億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超4萬1623張、估29.23億元。

4.友達(2409)收14.85元，跌0.75元或4.81%，成交量8.81萬張，外資賣超3萬1719張、估4.71億元，為連4日賣超，累計賣超9萬669張、估14.11億元。

5.鴻海(2317)收196.00元，跌7.00元或3.45%，成交量7.85萬張，外資賣超2萬8425張、估55.71億元，為連8日賣超，累計賣超19萬4254張、估403.97億元。

6.中石化(1314)收8.40元，跌0.09元或1.06%，成交量10.53萬張，外資賣超2萬5267張、估2.12億元，為連2日賣超，累計賣超3萬446張、估2.56億元。

7.台塑(1301)收44.75元，跌2.85元或5.99%，成交量4.76萬張，外資賣超1萬8932張、估8.47億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超3萬1461張、估14.46億元。

8.永豐金(2890)收31.20元，跌0.95元或2.95%，成交量3.11萬張，外資賣超1萬8054張、估5.63億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超2萬9295張、估9.2億元。

9.合庫金(5880)收22.55元，跌0.40元或1.74%，成交量2.45萬張，外資賣超1萬6624張、估3.75億元，為連3日賣超，累計賣超2萬9783張、估6.77億元。

10.臺企銀(2834)收14.85元，跌0.15元或1.00%，成交量3.08萬張，外資賣超1萬5872張、估2.36億元，為連16日賣超，累計賣超26萬3223張、估40.03億元。

●前三大個股近五日籌碼：

1.台新新光金(2887)三大法人累計賣超1萬3625張，其中外資賣超7萬8577張、投信買超5萬3409張、自營商買超1萬1542張；融資減2548張、融券減1張，券資比0.67%。

2.中鋼(2002)三大法人累計賣超6萬6496張，其中外資賣超6萬6670張、投信賣超1300張、自營商買超1474張；融資增2327張、融券增62張，券資比0.26%。

3.力積電(6770)三大法人累計買超2萬9312張，其中外資買超2萬1808張、投信賣超582張、自營商買超8086張；融資增2萬361張、融券增2958張，券資比5.02%。

註：台股總成交值不含鉅額交易；個股買賣超僅23日未加計鉅額交易；資券統計截至前一交易日。(編輯：龍彩霖)

• 外資賣超前十大個股中，台新新光金（2887）賣超張數最多，其次為中鋼（2002）、力積電（6770）。
• 賣超金額前三大為鴻海（2317）、力積電（6770）、台新新光金（2887）。
• 台新新光金已連續2日賣超。
• 外資已連續16日賣超臺企銀。

news.Title: 鴻海失守「郭董退休價」　郭台銘5天身價蒸發374億元
news.Content:
財經中心／楊惟甯報導

台股今（23）日在國際利空衝擊下重挫，電子權值股同步走弱，鴻海（2317）股價連續5個交易日收黑，正式摜破「郭董退休價」的200元大關，並失守年線，終場收在196元。在這波下殺潮中，持有大量持股的創辦人 郭台銘 身價同步縮水，短短5個交易日內蒸發約374億元，成為此波修正的最大受災戶。

受中東局勢惡化影響，美股20日四大指數全面收黑，帶動亞股今早同步重挫。台股開盤後一度大跌逾千點，權值股全面承壓，鴻海開盤即跌破200元關卡，盤中一度探低至195元，終場收在約196元附近。相較於法說會前的高點216.5元，短短五個交易日內已修正21.5元，累計跌幅達9.9%。

值得關注的是，200元大關被市場視為鴻海的重要多空分水嶺，此次失守是繼去年8月中旬以來、睽違7個月首見，也讓市場對短線走勢轉趨保守。

隨著股價兵敗如山倒，持股大戶郭台銘首當其衝！截至今年2月底，郭台銘個人持股高達174萬2199張，持股比例逾12%。以近5日股價下跌21.5元計算，其持股價值總計縮水約374.57億元。

儘管資產大幅波動，但根據《富比士》即時富豪榜數據，郭台銘在3月上旬的身價仍維持在153億美元（約新台幣4912億元）左右，穩坐台灣首富寶座。
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股23日重挫，鴻海（2317）股價連續5個交易日收黑，摜破200元大關，收在196元。
• 持有大量持股的創辦人郭台銘身價，短短5個交易日內蒸發約374.57億元。
• 200元大關被視為鴻海的重要多空分水嶺，此次失守是繼去年8月中旬以來、睽違7個月首見。
• 郭台銘個人持股高達174萬2199張，持股比例逾12%。
• 儘管資產波動，郭台銘身價仍維持在約4912億元，穩坐台灣首富寶座。
news.Keywords: 股價修正, 權值股, 創辦人持股, 價值蒸發, 多空分水嶺

news.Title: 《金融》亞股上周賣壓湧現 台股遭拋售最多
news.Content:
【時報記者林資傑台北報導】中東衝突局勢升溫，加劇市場對通膨擔憂，新興亞股上周整體淨流出109.1億美元，其中台股遭外資賣超56.5美元最多，其次為印度26.7億美元、南韓賣超22.1億美元，越南、泰國、菲律賓也遭外資拋售0.3～2.8億美元不等，亞股普遍賣壓籠罩。

指數漲跌部分，中國信託投信表示，上周亞股表現未與資金流向同步，指數呈現漲跌互見態勢，以南韓大漲5.4％稱冠、泰國漲幅1.7％居次，台股小漲0.4％，越南則下跌2.9％最重，菲律賓下跌0.7％、印尼下跌0.4％。

中國信託策略優利多重資產基金經理人羅世明表示，中東地緣政治風險持續攀升，上周國際油價持續創高，使通膨憂慮急遽升溫，加上市場偏鷹解讀上周聯準會、ECB等主要央行會議結果。值得留意的是，利率期貨顯示市場對聯準會今年降息預期已出現消退。

羅世明指出，目前市場僅聚焦高油價帶來的通膨風險，卻忽視高油價維持更久將侵蝕個人消費支出，伴隨金融情勢趨緊可能引發私募信貸等金融風險，經濟成長放緩也讓已經疲弱的美國就業成長面臨更大壓力。

整體而言，羅世明表示，今年聯準會降息預估維持不變，但基於短期油價大漲將推升通膨預期，預測降息時間點恐由6月延後至9月。

至於地緣政治對台股影響及後市觀察，中國信託台灣活力基金經理人徐啟葳指出，觀察過去以往8次地緣政治衝突，台股2周內平均下跌11％，但之後均彈回與上漲。

此外，外資將MSCI台灣指數2026年企業獲利成長預測調降3～29％，主要反應油價上升及GDP影響，不過仍維持對AI基礎建設供應鏈的正向看法，並認為能源及航運相關企業、電力及國防產業可能受益於本輪能源及地緣政治環境。

展望後市，徐啟葳認為，儘管短線股票漲多有技術性調整壓力與地緣政治風險，但台股在AI題材、台積電ADR溢價、高殖利率財報護航下，長線仍相對有撐，建議採逢低布局策略，持續留意AI相關電子題材股。
news.AI_Summary:
• 中東衝突局勢升溫，加劇市場對通膨擔憂，新興亞股上周整體淨流出109.1億美元。
• 台股遭外資賣超56.5億美元最多，其次為印度26.7億美元、南韓賣超22.1億美元。
• 上週亞股表現漲跌互見，南韓大漲5.4％稱冠，台股小漲0.4％。
• 市場對聯準會今年降息預期消退，預測降息時間點恐由6月延後至9月。
• MSCI台灣指數2026年企業獲利成長預測遭調降3～29%，但維持對AI基礎建設供應鏈的正向看法。
news.Keywords: 亞股資金流向, 外資賣超, 通膨擔憂, 降息預期, AI供應鏈

news.Title: Prediction: Nvidia Will Make a Substantial Dividend Increase in 2026. Should You Buy the Stock?
news.Content:
During Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) financial analyst question-and-answer session at GTC 2026, CEO Jensen Huang and CFO Colette Kress fielded a question about Nvidia's free cash flow (FCF) plans.

Huang answered first by saying that the primary uses of cash flow are the company's growth and Nvidia's ecosystem -- from its integrated hardware stack to supporting software. Beyond that, Nvidia will still generate significant FCF.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Kress then said that the company expects to use at least 50% of its FCF to return capital to Nvidia shareholders through buybacks and dividends -- especially in the second half of the year as Nvidia works through some of its more capital-intensive investments.

Nvidia didn't explicitly say it was raising its dividend. But with so much expected FCF, it would make a ton of sense. Here's why.

Nvidia's FCF is reaching unprecedented heights

In fiscal 2026, Nvidia earned $215.9 billion in revenue and $96.6 billion in FCF , which supported $41.1 billion in stock buybacks and dividends -- a combined 42.6% of FCF. So Nvidia is already committing a larger percentage of FCF to buybacks and dividends with its 50% target.

Analyst consensus estimates call for $8.28 in fiscal 2027 earnings per share, up from $4.90 in fiscal 2026. As a rough estimate, if we take that same growth rate of 69% and apply it to Nvidia's FCF, it would earn $163.3 billion in fiscal 2027 FCF. That translates to over $80 billion in projected buybacks and dividend payments.

Nvidia pays quarterly dividend of just $0.01 per share right now, which cost $974 million in fiscal 2026. So almost all of its capital return program is going toward buybacks. Other big tech-focused companies like Apple and Microsoft use a combination of buybacks and growing dividends to reward shareholders. And even Alphabet and Meta Platforms introduced dividends in 2024, although they are still significantly smaller than their buyback budgets.

The size of Nvidia's projected FCF and the precedent set by other mega-cap growth stocks are reason enough for Nvidia to boost its dividend. But the even better argument stems from the shift in its business model.

Monetizing mainstream AI

The data center infrastructure investment super cycle will eventually fade, which would be catastrophic if Nvidia were overly dependent on one-time hardware sales. But it isn't.

Story Continues

Nvidia's software is deeply embedded into its "extreme codesign" hardware, which is a rack-scale solution for artificial intelligence (AI) data centers that includes graphics processing units, central processing units, and associated networking hardware. Its CUDA software has been around for 20 years, and CUDA-powered systems have become widespread in AI data centers.

Nvidia's latest Feb. 25 earnings release and GTC event focused a lot on the inferencing inflection point, which is the shift from AI training to actually deploying and using that AI. Tokens are the currency for using AI models to do things -- like embedding agentic AI into enterprise workflows. It's a similar pricing structure to cloud services, where compute, storage, and networking are metered and billed based on usage.

Blackwell and Nvidia's latest Rubin architecture are tailor-made for low-latency, high-throughput inference to process as many tokens as possible. Tokens help Nvidia collect recurring revenue from its hardware in operation through enterprise software, support contracts, cloud servicing, and licensing for inference services. And if future Nvidia architectures continue to improve the token processing rate, hyperscalers will be incentivized to buy or rent that hardware.

A dividend is the logical next step for Nvidia

The inferencing boom should help Nvidia become less dependent on a spending supercycle and expand its recurring revenue base. That business model is well-suited to fund a sizable capital return program that remains primarily buybacks, but increasingly incorporates dividends as well.

If Nvidia pays a dividend, it could help grow its investor base by attracting folks who value passive income as an incentive to buy and hold a stock -- especially if Nvidia begins regularly raising its dividend each year, like Microsoft and Apple.

news.Title: Prediction: Nvidia Will Make a Substantial Dividend Increase in 2026. Should You Buy the Stock?
news.Content:
During Nvidia 's (NASDAQ: NVDA) financial analyst question-and-answer session at GTC 2026, CEO Jensen Huang and CFO Colette Kress fielded a question about Nvidia's free cash flow (FCF) plans.

Huang answered first by saying that the primary uses of cash flow are the company's growth and Nvidia's ecosystem -- from its integrated hardware stack to supporting software. Beyond that, Nvidia will still generate significant FCF.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Kress then said that the company expects to use at least 50% of its FCF to return capital to Nvidia shareholders through buybacks and dividends -- especially in the second half of the year as Nvidia works through some of its more capital-intensive investments.

Nvidia didn't explicitly say it was raising its dividend. But with so much expected FCF, it would make a ton of sense. Here's why.

Nvidia's FCF is reaching unprecedented heights

In fiscal 2026, Nvidia earned $215.9 billion in revenue and $96.6 billion in FCF , which supported $41.1 billion in stock buybacks and dividends -- a combined 42.6% of FCF. So Nvidia is already committing a larger percentage of FCF to buybacks and dividends with its 50% target.

Analyst consensus estimates call for $8.28 in fiscal 2027 earnings per share, up from $4.90 in fiscal 2026. As a rough estimate, if we take that same growth rate of 69% and apply it to Nvidia's FCF, it would earn $163.3 billion in fiscal 2027 FCF. That translates to over $80 billion in projected buybacks and dividend payments.

Nvidia pays quarterly dividend of just $0.01 per share right now, which cost $974 million in fiscal 2026. So almost all of its capital return program is going toward buybacks. Other big tech-focused companies like Apple and Microsoft use a combination of buybacks and growing dividends to reward shareholders. And even Alphabet and Meta Platforms introduced dividends in 2024, although they are still significantly smaller than their buyback budgets.

The size of Nvidia's projected FCF and the precedent set by other mega-cap growth stocks are reason enough for Nvidia to boost its dividend. But the even better argument stems from the shift in its business model.

Monetizing mainstream AI

The data center infrastructure investment super cycle will eventually fade, which would be catastrophic if Nvidia were overly dependent on one-time hardware sales. But it isn't.

Story Continues

Nvidia's software is deeply embedded into its "extreme codesign" hardware, which is a rack-scale solution for artificial intelligence (AI) data centers that includes graphics processing units, central processing units, and associated networking hardware. Its CUDA software has been around for 20 years, and CUDA-powered systems have become widespread in AI data centers.

Nvidia's latest Feb. 25 earnings release and GTC event focused a lot on the inferencing inflection point, which is the shift from AI training to actually deploying and using that AI. Tokens are the currency for using AI models to do things -- like embedding agentic AI into enterprise workflows. It's a similar pricing structure to cloud services, where compute, storage, and networking are metered and billed based on usage.

Blackwell and Nvidia's latest Rubin architecture are tailor-made for low-latency, high-throughput inference to process as many tokens as possible. Tokens help Nvidia collect recurring revenue from its hardware in operation through enterprise software, support contracts, cloud servicing, and licensing for inference services. And if future Nvidia architectures continue to improve the token processing rate, hyperscalers will be incentivized to buy or rent that hardware.

A dividend is the logical next step for Nvidia

The inferencing boom should help Nvidia become less dependent on a spending supercycle and expand its recurring revenue base. That business model is well-suited to fund a sizable capital return program that remains primarily buybacks, but increasingly incorporates dividends as well.

If Nvidia pays a dividend, it could help grow its investor base by attracting folks who value passive income as an incentive to buy and hold a stock -- especially if Nvidia begins regularly raising its dividend each year, like Microsoft and Apple.

news.Title: The Stock Market's Fear Index Is Up. Here's Why Smart Investors Aren't Selling.
news.Content:
With the war in Iran raging on, and questions abounding about whether the market is in an artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure bubble, investors are increasingly nervous. The CNN Fear and Greed Index has moved into new territory over the last month, going from a rating of 44, indicating slight fear, to 15, representing extreme fear.

The Fear and Greed Index looks at seven different market indicators to try to gauge the current mood of investors. When investors are fearful, it can drive down stock prices, and when they are greedy, stock prices tend to rise. Six of the seven indications the index uses are now in the extreme fear territory.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

This includes measuring market momentum by looking at whether the S&P 500 is below its 125-day moving average , which it currently is. It also looks at the ratio of stocks hitting new 52-week highs on the New York Stock Exchange versus lows. That number is just above break-even, which is also categorized as extreme fear. The index's measurements of stock breadth and put and call options also fall into the extreme fear category, as do junk bond demand and safe haven demand, which it measures as the 20-day difference between stock and Treasury returns. The only indicator not in extreme fear territory is market volatility, as measured by the VIX and its 50-day moving average, which is showing just fear.

The Fear and Greed Index is an interesting tool with regard to market sentiment. It's noteworthy that the two times in the past year when the index plunged into the single digits were great buying opportunities. That's why smart investors aren't selling.

This happened in early April 2025, before stocks went on a great run until autumn. The Fear Index then hit single digits again in late November, signaling another bottom with the market closing out the year strong. Given the adage to buy stocks when others are fearful, this price action makes sense, and the index appears to be doing its job.

So, what should investors do?

I think investors should stick to their overall strategy. For me, a core part of this is to continue to consistently dollar-cost average into an index exchange-traded fund (ETF) like the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSEMKT: VOO) . This fund tracks the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) , and has been a consistent market winner over time. In fact, less than 15% of actively managed funds have been able to beat the S&P 500 index over a 10-year period. The ETF is also a better vehicle for dollar-cost averaging than individual stocks because it naturally lets its winners rise to the top.

Story Continues

Meanwhile, I think investors who also like to supplement their investment portfolios with individual stocks can track the Fear and Greed Index and look to load up on some of their favorite stocks if the index once again hits single digits. You can track the index here .

news.Title: 《台北股市》重挫加碼誰？逾半ETF資金押寶0050
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美伊戰事持續影響全球股市，23日亞洲股市全面下挫，台股收盤重挫821點後，逢低加碼資金再度出籠，統計台股ETF單日成交金額排名前五，0050再以154.76億元居冠，三月以來已獲超過1,115億元資金流入，佔全體ETF逾半。

台股ETF 23日成交金額排行前五名中，續由市值型ETF龍頭 0050領軍，其次則為主動統一台股增長(00981A)、群益台灣精選(00919)、元大台灣50正2(00631L)與元大高股息(0056)。

法人指出，市場透過0050於台股重挫時進場已成趨勢，統計2025年以來至 2026/3/20，0050佔大盤及全體ETF日均成交金額分別為1.1%與11.3%，但今年台股跌幅前二的2026/3/9、2026/3/4，0050佔大盤及全體ETF日均成交金額比重，分別衝上4%與23%。

而三月以來，0050淨申購金額達1,115億元，佔台股ETF總淨申購金額55.2%，超過一半的流入資金是投入0050。

法人分析，雖美伊戰事造成震盪，但AI需求保持強勁，台股企業做為供應鏈中心，高度受惠全球產業架構，在出口強勁下包括國發會、主計總處連續上修台灣2026 GDP成長率預期。未來隨著戰事趨緩，資金重回軌道後，依過往歷史經驗將持續由龍頭權值股優先受惠。

0050擁有不錯過台股漲勢之特色，且在資金青睞權值股下，自AI起步成長的2023年以來至2026/3/20，臺灣50指數累積含息總報酬208.8%，勝出大盤含息報酬率160.7%約48個百分點。（新聞來源：工商即時 陳昱光）
news.AI_Summary:
• 台股ETF單日成交金額排行前五名，0050以154.76億元居冠，三月以來已獲超過1,115億元資金流入，佔全體ETF逾半。
• 市值型ETF龍頭0050在台股重挫時進場已成趨勢。
• 今年台股跌幅較大的日子，0050佔大盤及全體ETF日均成交金額比重顯著提升。
• 法人分析，AI需求強勁，台股企業作為供應鏈中心，出口強勁，GDP成長率預期上修。
• 預期未來隨著戰事趨緩，資金重回軌道後，龍頭權值股將優先受惠。
news.Keywords: ETF, 資金流入, 市值型ETF, 權值股, AI需求

news.Title: 《台股盤後》量縮收跌821點，失守33K
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-03-23 15:38:31 新聞中心 發佈

台股今(23)日開低走低，主要係美股四大指數上周五全面收黑、且早盤美股電子盤持續弱勢與原油期貨也因上週末川普談話而走高，輔以亞股或亞幣皆呈現外資匯出態勢，導致早盤一度跌逾千點，電子、金融及傳產族群幾乎全盤皆墨，僅剩造紙族群撐盤，惟近午盤台積電力守1800關卡、中華電等防禦型權值股也收撐盤之效，加權指數終場收在32722.50點，下跌821.38點或2.45%，成交量縮至6754.88億元。

隨著美國、以色列對伊朗的戰爭進入第四週，加深了通膨隱憂及升息疑慮，美國四大指數20日全面下挫。亞洲股市方面，日股週一下跌，市場因中東局勢進一步惡化，避險拋售情緒蔓延，日經225指數盤中寫下年內新低，收盤下跌3.48%；韓股週一也收低，KOSPI收盤下跌6.49%。

台股今日大幅回檔，主要因外資賣超與市場避險情緒升溫，電子、金融及傳產族群幾乎全面下挫，台積電一度失守1800元關卡，終場收在1810元，下跌1.63%，聯發科、台達電、日月光投控、緯穎等都有超過4%的跌幅，鴻海也跌逾3%。類股方面，造紙族群逆勢走強，受原材料價格或內需表現支撐，華紙漲逾6%。玻璃陶瓷類股跌幅最深，台玻、中釉都落入跌停，塑膠類股跌勢亦重，不過個股走勢分歧，受惠於原油價格飆漲，亞聚、台聚、台達化、華夏都亮燈漲停。

三大法人動向方面，今日三大法人總計賣超1046.27億元；其中，外資及陸資(不含外資自營商)賣超839.22億元，投信買超104.07億元，自營商賣超311.11億元。

台幣收盤貶幅收斂至3分，守住32元大關

《台股盤後》開高走高、收漲1342點，收復10日線

• 隨著美國、以色列對伊朗的戰爭進入第四週，加深了通膨隱憂及升息疑慮，美國四大指數20日全面下挫。
• 亞洲股市方面，日股週一下跌，市場因中東局勢進一步惡化，避險拋售情緒蔓延，日經225指數盤中寫下年內新低，收盤下跌3.48%；韓股週一也收低，KOSPI收盤下跌6.49%。
• 台股今日大幅回檔，主要因外資賣超與市場避險情緒升溫，電子、金融及傳產族群幾乎全面下挫，台積電一度失守1800元關卡，終場收在1810元，下跌1.63%，聯發科、台達電、日月光投控、緯穎等都有超過4%的跌幅，鴻海也跌逾3%。
• 類股方面，造紙族群逆勢走強，受原材料價格或內需表現支撐，華紙漲逾6%。玻璃陶瓷類股跌幅最深，台玻、中釉都落入跌停，塑膠類股跌勢亦重，不過個股走勢分歧，受惠於原油價格飆漲，亞聚、台聚、台達化、華夏都亮燈漲停。
• 三大法人動向方面，今日三大法人總計賣超1046.27億元；其中，外資及陸資(不含外資自營商)賣超839.22億元，投信買超104.07億元，自營商賣超311.11億元。
• 台幣收盤貶幅收斂至3分，守住32元大關
news.Keywords: 地緣政治風險, 賣壓, 法人賣超, 權值股, 避險

news.Title: Skip the IPO Wait: How to Invest in SpaceX Today
news.Content:
Unless market turmoil ruins the fun, 2026 looks to be a banner year for initial public offerings ( IPOs ). Giant private companies such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and Databricks are planning to sell stock to public investors.

No IPO is as anticipated as SpaceX. The spaceflight company founded by Elon Musk has been private for two decades and is rumored to be planning the largest IPO in history later this year at a valuation of $1.5 trillion or higher.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

The downside of an IPO is that retail investors may only be able to buy SpaceX stock after a potential IPO pop, putting them at the back of the line. Luckily, investors have a workaround to buying SpaceX stock today.

Here's how anyone -- not just professional investors -- can get exposure to SpaceX stock before its upcoming market debut.

A publicly traded tracking stock

SpaceX has been making massive investments to expand its Starlink satellite internet service, with plans to add more capacity and eventually offer direct-to-mobile-device connectivity (today, you need a terminal to use it). This is one of the reasons it wants to raise massive amounts of capital through an IPO: It will be expensive to send all these satellites into orbit.

It is also the reason the company made a massive deal with EchoStar (NASDAQ: SATS) . The telecommunications provider, which also owns Dish Network and Boost Mobile, sold its spectrum to SpaceX in a massive deal valued at $19.5 billion. In phased transactions, EchoStar will receive $8.5 billion in cash and $11 billion in SpaceX stock, with SpaceX funding EchoStar's interest payments on its massive debt load.

SpaceX needed EchoStar's frequencies (the spectrum) in order to beam internet directly to mobile devices, and it paid a pretty penny for it. Now, EchoStar can almost be treated as a proxy for owning SpaceX shares. The company does have a lot of debt, but it will also receive $8.5 billion in cash from SpaceX and coverage of its loan interest payments. Along with the value from its legacy assets, EchoStar can likely clear its debt obligations without considering monetizing its SpaceX investment.

When SpaceX gave EchoStar shares in its business, the spaceflight giant was valued at around $400 billion. Even if we factor in any shareholder dilution associated with the IPO, this investment may be worth three or four times its $11 billion cost basis by the time SpaceX goes public. This is why EchoStar stock is up 300% over the past year, with a market cap of over $30 billion.

Story Continues

Investing in SpaceX does not come without risks

Buying EchoStar stock right now can roughly get you exposure to SpaceX stock pre-IPO. This will obviously excite any retail investor, given how sought-after SpaceX shares are.

However, at a valuation of $1.5 trillion or more, any investor needs to consider whether SpaceX is worth owning in their portfolio. I think it's a risky bet at this price.

First, SpaceX now owns X (formerly Twitter) and xAI. Twitter is reportedly losing users at a steady rate after the Elon Musk acquisition, while xAI burns through tons of capital to build out its AI capabilities, still with close to zero market share compared to big players such as OpenAI or Anthropic. These will create a liability for SpaceX after it goes public.

Second, SpaceX stock may be expensive at this valuation, even with its massive growth potential. Last year, the company reported revenue of around $15 billion. That would give it a price-to-sales ratio (P/S) of around 100, making it one of, if not the most expensive, large-cap stocks in history. Even if revenue quadruples to $60 billion and the company has a 30% profit margin (unlikely given the business's capital intensity), that would yield $18 billion in earnings and a price-to-earnings ratio (P/E) of 83. This is an expensive earnings ratio, and SpaceX will likely not generate this level of earnings for many years.

Just because you can get exposure to SpaceX stock before the IPO does not mean you should.

Don’t miss this second chance at a potentially lucrative opportunity

Ever feel like you missed the boat in buying the most successful stocks? Then you’ll want to hear this.

On rare occasions, our expert team of analysts issues a “Double Down” stock recommendation for companies that they think are about to pop. If you’re worried you’ve already missed your chance to invest, now is the best time to buy before it’s too late. And the numbers speak for themselves:

Nvidia: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2009, you’d have $447,961 !*

Apple: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2008, you’d have $47,222 !*

Netflix: if you invested $1,000 when we doubled down in 2004, you’d have $495,179 !*

Right now, we’re issuing “Double Down” alerts for three incredible companies , available when you join Stock Advisor , and there may not be another chance like this anytime soon.

See the 3 stocks »

*Stock Advisor returns as of March 16, 2026

Brett Schafer has no position in any of the stocks mentioned. The Motley Fool has no position in any of the stocks mentioned. The Motley Fool has a disclosure policy .

news.Title: 《台北股市》雙資大逃殺！狂砍破千億、居今年前5大 前低恐不保？
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】美伊互槓致中東戰事升級，油價居高引發通膨恐慌，日韓股大跳水同聲哀號，台幣早盤貶破32元大關，台股雙王帶頭殺，權王台積電1800告急，電子權值股慘遭狙擊，指數盤中狂瀉1082點，終場下挫821點，同寫今年第三慘案，並分居史上第8及11大！今日外資(不含外資自營商)賣超839.226億元，投信買超104.0721億元，自營商賣超311.1166億元，三大法人賣超1046.27億元。

觀察籌碼面，中東戰事加劇，外資大舉撤出台股，連3周賣超逾千億元，上周共計減碼1655億，受累傳出FED考慮升息，激勵美元上漲，台幣貶破32元大關，今日於台股上演大逃殺提款839.22億，居今年第五大、史上11大刀，連3日賣超達2151.81億元，累計3月來拋售逾8044億，持續改寫歷史高！投信連2買、今日加碼百億居今年第五大，成台股最強靠山，自營商今日賣超居今年第三大、史上第六大，連3天累計出脫523.5億元。

中東戰爭進入第四周，美伊叫囂你來我往，戰火波及中東產油地區，加上荷姆茲海峽遭伊朗強力封鎖，油價居高不下，全球股市腥風血雨，台股早盤殺聲隆隆崩跌千點，電子權值股遭血洗，DRAM、PCB、低軌衛星等成殺盤重心，雙王領頭下殺，台積電上演1800關保衛戰，鴻海更丟失200關，終場加權指數收黑821.38點、報32722.5點，成交值縮為6754.88億元。

盤中跌停股不乏人氣熱門股，包括記憶體的威剛(3260)、創見(2451)、晶豪科(3006)、廣穎(4973)、宜鼎(5289)、宇瞻(8271)，PCB的欣興(3037)、楠梓電(2316)、尖點(8021)，矽光子的上詮(3363)、統新(6426)、九豪(6127)，低軌衛星的萊德光電-KY(7717)、位速(3508)、正文(4906)等。

戰爭概念股成資金避風港，受惠油價飆升的台聚家族台聚(1304)、亞聚(1308)、台達化(1309)及華夏(1305)攜手欣高(9931)、欣天然(9918)全面亮燈漲停，台塑四寶台塑(1301)、南亞(1303)、台化(1326)、台塑化(6505)沒討到糖吃全數收黑。

分析師表示，台股今日跳空殺低再度摜破月線支撐，短線技術面轉弱，下跌缺口未回補前，仍有測試前波低檔可能，操作宜偏保守因應，個股確實依短線技術面變化因應，嚴格做好資金控管。

此外，受累伊朗持續攻擊鄰國煉油及天然氣設施，導致油價居高不下，弱化年底前FED降息預期，公債殖利率急升造成美股跳水下修，台股也難倖免，下檔支撐32000點到32500點需守住，否則將向下尋求3萬點整數關卡支撐，若遇較大回檔時，可留意產業能見度較高的AI供應鏈逢低上車機會。(編輯：龍彩霖)
news.AI_Summary:
• 美伊衝突升級，油價居高引發通膨恐慌，日韓股大跳水，台幣貶破32元大關，台股雙王帶頭殺，指數盤中狂瀉1082點，終場下挫821點，為今年第三慘案。
• 外資(不含自營商)賣超839.226億元，投信買超104.0721億元，自營商賣超311.1166億元，三大法人賣超1046.27億元。
• 外資連3周賣超逾千億元，上週減碼1655億，累計3月來拋售逾8044億。
• 投信連2買，今日加碼百億，成台股最強靠山。
• 記憶體、PCB、低軌衛星等成殺盤重心，台積電上演1800關保衛戰，鴻海失守200關。
news.Keywords: 地緣政治風險, 外資賣超, 投信買超, 權值股, 恐慌性賣壓

news.Title: 【Follow法人】外資賣超839億元創史上第11大！狂砍0050逾8萬張賣超 台積電也遭殃
news.Content:
（下午5時45分更新外資買賣超個股）

台股今（23）日失守3萬3千點關卡，三大法人合計賣超1046億元，其中外資大砍839.22億元，創史上第11大賣超紀錄；累計3月以來，外資賣超已高達8510.97億元。資深台股分析師王兆立表示，外資大舉調節主要反映國際戰事升溫與市場不確定性，短線資金轉趨保守。觀察外資買賣超，賣超集中在0050，大砍逾8.7萬張登今日賣超王，台積電（2330）也遭砍1.5萬張賣超。

外資今日賣超前三大：0050、台新新光金、中鋼

今日賣超榜外資以元大台灣50（0050）居冠，今外資大砍逾8.7萬張，慘淪賣超第一；其次則是台新新光金（2887）拿下，賣超逾7.2萬張，第三則是中鋼（2002）也遭外資砍逾4.6萬張。

值得注意的是，面板股友達（2409）也同步遭砍逾3.1萬張；鴻海（2317），也賣超逾2.8萬張，排進今日賣超第六大，此外台積電也遭殃，遭外資砍1.5萬張。

外資今日買超前三大：長榮航、華邦電、大聯大

今日買超方面以長榮航（2618）居冠，獲外資掃貨逾2外資買盤重心，以及記憶體族群華邦電（2344）則獲得逾1.3萬張買超，排第二；大聯大（3702）今獲外資青睞逾萬張買超，居第三。

資深台股分析師王兆立表示，外資大幅調節主要受到國際戰事升溫與市場不確定性影響，短線資金轉趨保守。

王兆立指出，近期國際戰爭因素干擾市場情緒，加上美股同步進入修正階段，全球股市普遍走弱，外資因此選擇降低風險部位，「在不確定性升高的情況下，外資偏向站在賣方」。此外，市場對於後續局勢發展仍難以預測，也使資金持續撤出股市觀望。

觀察法人動向，投信則相對偏多操作，王兆立認為，可能與ETF相關布局或特定資金進場有關；至於自營商也同步擴大賣超，反映市場整體風險意識升高，「不只是外資，連內資與一般投資人也偏向減碼」。

王兆立表示，短線盤勢仍將高度受到戰爭情勢影響，只要市場對局勢轉趨悲觀，外資賣超壓力恐持續存在。他建議投資人，應密切觀察外資動向，尤其是現貨賣壓是否縮小，以及期貨空單是否減少，作為判斷市場回穩的重要指標。

三大法人賣破千億元 自營商單日砍311億元寫史上第六大

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計賣超1046億元，外資今大砍839.22億元，創下史上第十大賣超金額紀錄。

自3月以來，外資合計賣超金額達8510.97億元；投信今日則是買超104.07億元。

自營商單日合計賣超311.1億元寫史上第六大，其中自行買賣部分賣超60.24億元，避險方面賣超250.86億元。

台股今日開低走低，終場收漲821點，收在32,722點，成交量7,114億元。台積電（2330）收盤價1,810元，下跌30元。

湯暖萱／財經特派記者，主跑主計處、營建、百貨紡織等

news.Title: 【Follow法人】外資賣超839億元創史上第11大！狂砍0050逾8萬張賣超 台積電也遭殃
news.Content:
（下午5時45分更新外資買賣超個股）

台股今（23）日失守3萬3千點關卡，三大法人合計賣超1046億元，其中外資大砍839.22億元，創史上第11大賣超紀錄；累計3月以來，外資賣超已高達8510.97億元。資深台股分析師王兆立表示，外資大舉調節主要反映國際戰事升溫與市場不確定性，短線資金轉趨保守。觀察外資買賣超，賣超集中在0050，大砍逾8.7萬張登今日賣超王，台積電（2330）也遭砍1.5萬張賣超。

外資今日賣超前三大：0050、台新新光金、中鋼

今日賣超榜外資以元大台灣50（0050）居冠，今外資大砍逾8.7萬張，慘淪賣超第一；其次則是台新新光金（2887）拿下，賣超逾7.2萬張，第三則是中鋼（2002）也遭外資砍逾4.6萬張。

值得注意的是，面板股友達（2409）也同步遭砍逾3.1萬張；鴻海（2317），也賣超逾2.8萬張，排進今日賣超第六大，此外台積電也遭殃，遭外資砍1.5萬張。

外資今日買超前三大：長榮航、華邦電、大聯大

今日買超方面以長榮航（2618）居冠，獲外資掃貨逾2外資買盤重心，以及記憶體族群華邦電（2344）則獲得逾1.3萬張買超，排第二；大聯大（3702）今獲外資青睞逾萬張買超，居第三。

資深台股分析師王兆立表示，外資大幅調節主要受到國際戰事升溫與市場不確定性影響，短線資金轉趨保守。

王兆立指出，近期國際戰爭因素干擾市場情緒，加上美股同步進入修正階段，全球股市普遍走弱，外資因此選擇降低風險部位，「在不確定性升高的情況下，外資偏向站在賣方」。此外，市場對於後續局勢發展仍難以預測，也使資金持續撤出股市觀望。

觀察法人動向，投信則相對偏多操作，王兆立認為，可能與ETF相關布局或特定資金進場有關；至於自營商也同步擴大賣超，反映市場整體風險意識升高，「不只是外資，連內資與一般投資人也偏向減碼」。

王兆立表示，短線盤勢仍將高度受到戰爭情勢影響，只要市場對局勢轉趨悲觀，外資賣超壓力恐持續存在。他建議投資人，應密切觀察外資動向，尤其是現貨賣壓是否縮小，以及期貨空單是否減少，作為判斷市場回穩的重要指標。

三大法人賣破千億元 自營商單日砍311億元寫史上第六大

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計賣超1046億元，外資今大砍839.22億元，創下史上第十大賣超金額紀錄。

自3月以來，外資合計賣超金額達8510.97億元；投信今日則是買超104.07億元。

自營商單日合計賣超311.1億元寫史上第六大，其中自行買賣部分賣超60.24億元，避險方面賣超250.86億元。

台股今日開低走低，終場收漲821點，收在32,722點，成交量7,114億元。台積電（2330）收盤價1,810元，下跌30元。

湯暖萱／財經特派記者，主跑主計處、營建、百貨紡織等

news.Title: 電子權值股倒一片　台股終場下跌821點
news.Content:
[NOWnews今日新聞] 台北股市今（23）日迎來拉回賣壓，開盤下跌209.88點、來到33334點，盤中最低一度來到32461.09點，下跌1082.79點，終場下跌821.38點或2.45%，收在32722.5點，跌破10日線，成交量為6754.88億元。

中小型股同步走低，櫃買指數開盤下跌1.71點、來到326.69點，隨後同步走低，終場下跌11.38點或3.47%，收在317.02點，跌破短期均線5日、10日線成交量為1884.42億元。今日個股成交量排行榜前五名為：群創、力積電、菱生、華邦電、台新新光金；個股成交值排行榜前五名為：台積電、台達電、南亞科、力積電、華邦電。

權值股全數走低，權王台積電以1790元開出，盤中最低一度來到1785元，不過隨後有低接買盤挹注，站回1800元之上，終場上漲30元或1.63%，收在1810元，拖累大盤指數就有244點；台達電同樣開低走低，終場下跌65元或4.41%，收在1410元，拖累大盤指數就有48點；鴻海終場下跌7元或3.45%，收在196元，拖累大盤指數有34點。

先前強勁的熱門股今日也迎來拉回，PCB族群一片綠油油，欣興、台玻、尖點、柏承、楠梓電被打到跌停板，金居下跌逾9%，景碩、泰鼎-KY、鉅橡下跌逾8%，榮科下跌逾7%，高技、台虹、新復興、凱崴下跌逾6%。

記憶體族群也慘遭提款，力積電下跌逾8%，旺宏下跌逾7%，南亞科下跌逾4%，華邦電下跌逾2%；連帶模組廠也掀起跌停潮，宜鼎、廣穎、威剛、創見、宇瞻被打到跌停板，至上下跌逾7%，凌航、群聯下跌逾6%，品安下跌逾5%。

不過資金也轉向避險資產，其中表現最強勁的就是造紙族群，類股指數是今日唯一逆勢走高的，上漲逾2%，華紙上漲逾6%，正隆上漲逾4%，永豐餘上漲逾2%。

由於中東地緣政治升溫，導致國際油價有望再度走高，資金也持續卡位塑化股，亞聚、台聚、台達化、華夏攻上漲停板，東聯上漲逾2%。
news.AI_Summary:

• 櫃買指數終場下跌11.38點或3.47%，收在317.02點。
• 權王台積電終場收在1810元，下跌30元，拖累大盤指數約244點。
• PCB族群一片綠油油，欣興、台玻、尖點等被打到跌停板。
• 造紙族群逆勢走高，類股指數上漲逾2%，亞聚、台聚、台達化、華夏攻上漲停板。

news.Title: 戰火恐慌最重殺1082點! 台股跌821點收32722
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者謝旻蓁,攝影詹明樺

台股又見黑色星期一，在中東衝突延燒下，台股開盤大跌超過千點，失守33000點整數大關，電子類股全面走弱，台積電最低下跌55元來到1785元，終場下跌30元收在1810元，鴻海跌破200元，收場收在196元，台達電、聯發科也有明顯跌幅，資金避風港聚焦在戰爭相關概念股，像是亞聚、台聚、欣天然等皆亮燈漲停，另外像是萬潤、弘塑等半導體設備概念股也湧現資金進駐，大盤指數終場下跌821點，收在32722點，成交量達6754億元。

民眾：「比較沒那麼在意，會再漲回來，基本上都在科技股，有南亞科然後台積電之類的，看有沒有跌破5日均線，或是月均線，有跌破就是止損。」

民眾：「我覺得可預期，就是之前很高，所以現在有點回彈，也是很正常，前兩周就大概做好準備，就有稍微出一些，就不會留這麼多。」

美伊隔空交火升級，投資人風險調控情緒再度反映在股市當中，台股周一（23）開盤大跌逾千點，最低觸及32461點，隨後低接買盤進場，跌幅逐漸收斂，但仍跌破月線，失守33000點大關。

統一投顧董事長 黎方國：「整個地緣政治的狀況，並沒有緩和，所以今天台股就是，直接就開低走低，而且持續下殺，而且整個多頭的線型受到破壞，目前是跌破月線，而且是整個月線往下彎曲，今天主要的類股都下跌，半導體類股是帶頭下殺的主要類股，比較好的狀況是傳產類股，相對之前沒有什麼漲到，抗漲的股票今天也相對抗跌。」

觀察盤面走勢，電子類股普遍走弱，台積電最低下跌55元來到1785元，後續在1800元附近整理；鴻海跌破200元整數大關，台達電、聯發科等大型電子權值股也成為外資提款機；記憶體族群多檔跌停，金融、航運也未能吸引買盤進駐。市場資金持續流入戰爭相關概念股，像是塑化股、天然氣概念股等；萬潤、弘塑等台積電設備概念股也吸引資金卡位。

專家分析，油價仍是這波股市重挫的導火線，後續觀察重點落在融資餘額水位，以及外資資金流向是否傳遞止穩訊號。

富邦投顧董事長 陳奕光：「油價落在120（美元）到130（美元），可能指數就會去挑戰，前波這部分的低點31529（點），油價再升破130來到150，大概130到150，那可能就會去挑戰整數關卡。新台幣在這個地方貶到32塊，假如說能夠止貶回升，那外資的賣超其實就會止住；看到單日的融資減到100億以上，那這也是一個止跌訊號。」

布局策略上，專家建議可留意季線的支撐力道，分批進場降低風險，同時聚焦在塑化類股、晶圓代工概念股，以及生技類股等領域，掌握市場輪動所帶來的投資契機。（記者謝旻蓁、詹明樺／台北採訪報導）

專家分析，油價仍是這波股市重挫的導火線，後續觀察重點落在融資餘額水位，以及外資資金流向是否傳遞止穩訊號。

富邦投顧董事長 陳奕光：「油價落在120（美元）到130（美元），可能指數就會去挑戰，前波這部分的低點31529（點），油價再升破130來到150，大概130到150，那可能就會去挑戰整數關卡。新台幣在這個地方貶到32塊，假如說能夠止貶回升，那外資的賣超其實就會止住；看到單日的融資減到100億以上，那這也是一個止跌訊號。」

布局策略上，專家建議可留意季線的支撐力道，分批進場降低風險，同時聚焦在塑化類股、晶圓代工概念股，以及生技類股等領域，掌握市場輪動所帶來的投資契機。（記者謝旻蓁、詹明樺／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 魏哲家批「中國機器人」飄了？她揭昔日董座「2霸氣發言」網笑： 站著賺
news.Content:
財經中心／曾郁雅報導

台積電董事長魏哲家近日開酸「中國機器人」跳來跳去、沒有用，點出晶片才是科技發展核心、台積電才是扮演重要角色，發言引發外界討論，財信傳媒董事長謝金河就認為此段發言有其「底氣與厚度」，不過財經網紅「不敗教主 」陳重銘則認為魏哲家此段發言「飄了」（中國網路用語：太自滿），對此，財經網美Emmy發文分析，認為魏哲家的說法其實點出業界普遍共識，並回顧台積電歷任領導人向來發言風格皆相當直接「脫口大實話」的應對，讓不少網友看了笑出來：「人家站在世界科技頂端的人看世界難道需要被質疑？」。

財經網美Emmy認為，此說法其實反映產業現況，許多展示型機器人仍需大量人工調校。（圖／翻攝自微博）

「財經網美」Emmy發文提及近日魏哲家公開談中國機器人「沒有用、好看頭而已」，沒想到卻被評為「飄了」、「領導人過度自滿，是企業的大忌」類似言論，讓她忍不住跳出來分析，中國春晚機器人表演之所以能呈現流暢效果，背後往往需要大量工程師長時間調校程式與動作，透過反覆測試才能確保演出順利完成，但若臨場要求機器人即時變換動作，仍可能面臨技術限制，她認為，目前全球人形機器人發展仍在快速演進階段，產業普遍關注特斯拉Optimus等產品的進展，未來技術成熟後，各國才有機會加速跟進，他認為此領域確實全世界都在等馬斯克發話，更笑稱：「我感覺馬斯克是不是比較飄？」。

Emmy也回顧張忠謀曾回應「你買不起」、劉德音喊出「買台積電」，直言歷任董座發言向來直接，並反問「魏哲家有很囂張嗎？」。（圖／民視資料照）

針對外界批評魏哲家「口氣太大」，甚至不如張忠謀等評論，Emmy也回顧台積電過往領導人的發言風格，她提到，2015年中國紫光集團董事長趙偉國曾表態有意收購台積電與聯發科，創辦人張忠謀當時回應「你買不起」，展現強勢態度；而前董事長劉德音在2024年退休前出席股東會時，當時台積電已經接近一千元，也依舊喊出「買台積電」，同樣引發市場討論。

<figcaption class="text-px14 text-dolphin mt-2 wrap-anywhere" st

• 台積電董事長魏哲家近期批評中國機器人「沒用、好看而已」，認為晶片才是科技發展核心。
• 財信傳媒董事長謝金河認為此發言有「底氣與厚度」，但財經網紅陳重銘認為魏哲家「飄了」，過度自滿。
• 財經網美Emmy分析，魏哲家的說法反映業界共識，許多機器人仍需大量人工調校。
• Emmy回顧台積電歷任領導人發言風格皆相當直接，如張忠謀曾說「你買不起」、劉德音喊出「買台積電」。
• 網友認為魏哲家身處世界科技頂端，其發言不應被質疑。

自營商賣超311.12億元，投信買超104.07億元，外資及陸資賣超839.22億元。1150323

news.Title: 台股黑色星期一終場跌821點　逾千檔股票下跌
news.Content:
受中東情勢緊張影響，市場避險情緒升高，全球股債同步遭到拋售，美股週五（20日）再重挫，台股今（23日）開盤急殺逾3%，狂跌1048.55點至32495.33點，隨後跌點漸收斂至千點內，終場收在32,722.50點，下跌821.38點，下跌家數來到1103檔。

今日焦點個股表現，台積電跌30元，收在1810元，跌幅1.63%；鴻海下跌7元，跌幅3.45%，至196元；聯發科下跌75元至1625元；廣達下跌3.5元來到280元；長榮下跌10元至204元；記憶體族群南亞科、華邦電下挫，旺宏重挫逾11%。

美股四大主要指數在前一交易日全面下跌，道瓊工業指數終場下跌443.96點或0.96％，收45577.47點；標準普爾500指數下跌100.01點或1.51％，收6506.48點；那斯達克指數下跌443.08點或2.01％，收在21647.61點；費城半導體指數下跌192.69點或2.45％，收7670.61點。（責任編輯：卓琦）

• 受中東情勢緊張影響，市場避險情緒升高，全球股債同步遭到拋售，美股週五（20日）重挫。
• 台股23日開盤急殺逾3%，狂跌1048.55點至32495.33點，終場收在32,722.50點，下跌821.38點。
• 台積電跌30元，收在1810元；鴻海下跌7元，至196元；聯發科下跌75元至1625元。
• 記憶體族群南亞科、華邦電下挫，旺宏重挫逾11%。
• 美股四大主要指數在前一交易日全面下跌，道瓊工業指數下跌0.96%。

news.Title: 《台北股市》台股跌破33K 散戶信心潰散？3大利多點燃續攻動能
news.Content:
【時報-台北電】最新公布的台灣元月景氣燈號續亮紅燈，國發會直指正是受惠於AI需求與民間消費力道增加，帶動景氣穩定成長。在產業、獲利、政策三大利多助威下，統一投信看好台股向上動能可期。

台灣企業獲利呈現正成長，統一投顧指出，根據彭博統計，去（2025年）年台灣上市公司每股盈餘成長率將高達15％，突顯去年台股指數向上走，是有基本面支撐的「有基之彈」；預估2026年獲利成長率更有機會衝上48.6％，可望創下近年新高，將有利吸引資金進場，推升台股成長動能。

統一投信指出，AI發展驅動相關供應鏈進化，台灣身為全球AI硬體製造中心，產業技術持續提升，推動企業獲利表現上揚，看好AI產業中包含網通/高速傳輸、AI伺服器，以及人形機器人等領域，未來商機將相當可觀。

台股基本面長線有撐，資金面亦有望迎接聯準會（Fed）降息循環所帶來的資金活水。雖因中東戰事影響，美國通膨降溫速度恐有所停滯，可能導致Fed降息步調與幅度受到部分壓抑；但市場預期，全年仍將處於降息循環中。只要資金流動活絡，股市多頭行情就值得期待。

從產業升級、企業獲利和全球貨幣政策趨勢來看，台股投資價值已不可同日而語。

統一投信繼旗下首檔台股主動式ETF 00981A規模至3月20已突破938億元、逼近千億元之後，將再推出「統一台股升級50主動式（00403A）ETF，IPO入手價每張1萬元、即每單位10元，布局策略擬結合市值投概念與主動選股，引發關注。

該檔預計4月22日至24日募集繳款，選股強調主動汰弱增強，致力開創提升投資效益的機會，以幫助投資人共享台灣產業成長紅利。

00403A擬任經理人張哲瑋表示，為打造台股升級50組合，持股配置預計以市值前50大企業為核心，並放大選股範圍至市值前200大企業*，作為主要增強選股池，藉由汰除弱勢股，增持強勢股比重，以期強化投資效能。（新聞來源：工商即時 呂淑美）
news.AI_Summary:
• 台灣元月景氣燈號續亮紅燈，受惠於AI需求與民間消費力道增加。
• 統一投顧預估2026年台灣上市公司獲利成長率有機會衝上48.6％，創近年新高。
• 看好AI產業中的網通/高速傳輸、AI伺服器、人形機器人等領域商機。
• 儘管中東戰事影響通膨，但預期全年仍處於降息循環中，資金流動活絡有利股市多頭。
• 統一投信將推出「統一台股升級50主動式（00403A）ETF」，布局策略結合市值與主動選股。

news.Title: 台股收跌821點「史上11慘」！跌幅2.4％優於中日韓股　法人這樣說
news.Content: 中東情勢升溫，美伊持續叫陣，互不退讓，國際油價居高不下，導致國際股市氣氛偏空，美股上週五走跌，那斯達克指數、費城半導體指數跌幅都超過2％，驚嚇到今天開盤的亞洲股市，截至發稿為止，韓股大跌6.5％，日股下跌3.4％，上證指數、港股跌幅也都超過3％。 回頭看台股，今天最低曾重挫1082點，來到32461點，收盤仍下跌821點，是史上單日第11大收盤跌點，不過，跌幅2.4％，相對比中日韓股市要輕。 受到台積電ADR下跌2.8％的牽連，台積電今天盤中一度跌至1785元，跌幅3％，收盤仍下跌1.6％，收在1810元。 展望盤勢，「鉅亨買基金」表示，此次重挫關鍵來自中東戰事升溫與私募信貸疑慮雙重衝擊，不過，私募市場本就非一般投資人可直接參與標的，無須過度恐慌。面對震盪，投資人更應回到自身投資節奏，避免情緒干擾長期布局。 「鉅亨買基金」總經理張榮仁分析，戰事未來可能出現三種情境：首先是戰術性降溫，若局勢緩解，油價可望回落；其次為有限升級，在控制戰線下維持高檔震盪；第三則是極限施壓，若能源供應遭破壞，油價恐大幅飆升，市場波動也將顯著放大。 張榮仁表示，若衝突外溢至能源運輸命脈，通膨壓力將再起，聯準會短期內更難轉向寬鬆；反之，若戰事受控，市場仍有機會逐步回穩。因此，觀察能源設施是否成為主要攻擊目標，以及荷莫茲海峽通行情況，將是判斷市場方向的重要指標。 除了地緣政治風險，市場也關注另一隻「灰犀牛」：私募信貸的流動性壓力。近期大型機構旗下私募信貸基金出現贖回壓力與虧損，引發市場警覺。張榮仁指出，私募信貸本身具有槓桿高、流動性低與資訊不透明等特性，在高利率環境下，風險確實容易累積。現在市場在意的，主要是高利率環境如果維持更久，可能讓私募市場的壓力逐步浮現，但這不代表一般公募基金會直接面臨同樣程度的衝擊。 張榮仁說，公募基金投資標的以流動性高、資訊透明的資產為主，與私募市場連動性有限，投資人不需過度放大風險。真正需要關注的是油價與利率走勢對市場評價的影響，當市場從預期降息轉為適應高利率環境時，資產配置與投資節奏就變得更重要。 近日投資市場波動，TISA級別基金仍有強勁表現，上周績效前十含息報酬率落在7％～9％，遠高於大盤同期的0.43%，其中，凱基台灣精五門基金-新臺幣TISA、新光大三通基金-TISA雙雙繳出逾9%的績效，前者更創下今年以來最高淨值。 拉長時間來看，台灣近一月績效呈現負報酬，這十檔TISA級別基金績效更全數站上雙位數。 凱基台灣精五門基金研究團隊表示，本次FOMC會議結論大致符合市場預期，即便中東戰事為經濟前景增添了不確定性，但聯準會仍上調2026年整年的經濟增長率。且根據最新資料顯示，預期S&P 500指數企業2026年盈餘增長上看15.3%，企業獲利增長仍為推升股市的主要動能，受惠AI發展的台廠供應鏈後市仍值得期待。
news.AI_Summary:
• 受中東戰事升溫影響，台股23日下跌821點，跌幅2.4％，相對比中日韓股市要輕。
• 台積電ADR下跌2.8％，台積電盤中一度跌至1785元，收盤下跌1.6％，收在1810元。
• 法人分析，此次重挫關鍵來自中東戰事升溫與私募信貸疑慮，但公募基金受影響有限。
• 觀察重點為油價與利率走勢對市場評價的影響，以及荷姆茲海峽通行情況。
• TISA級別基金表現強勁，近一月績效遠高於大盤，顯示企業獲利增長仍是推升股市動能。
news.Keywords: 地緣政治風險, 股市波動, 油價, 利率走勢, AI供應鏈

news.Title: 【盤後焦點】台股量縮下跌821點　台積電守住1800元關卡
news.Content:
（中央社記者鍾榮峰台北2026年3月23日電）中東衝突未見轉機，能源供給憂慮升高，台股早盤下挫1082.79點，中場跌幅收斂、量縮震盪，台積電 (2330) 等權值股、高價股普遍弱勢，金融、中小型股拉回，終場加權指數收32722.5點，下跌821.38點，跌幅2.45%。

台股今天呈現弱勢整理，成交值量縮為新台幣6754.88億元。電子指數跌2.5%，金融指數跌1.5%；代表中小型股的櫃買OTC指數終場挫跌3.47%，跌破10日均線317.6點。

股價超過千元的高價股漲跌互見，高價股守住「37千金」，股王信驊 (5274) 收11465元，跌285元，跌幅2.43%。

權王台積電終場收1810元，下跌30元，跌幅1.63%，市值46.93兆元。其他電子權值股台達電 (2308) 終場收1410元，跌4.41%；鴻海 (2317) 跌破200元大關，收196元，跌3.45%；聯發科 (2454) 收1625元，跌4.41%；日月光投控 (3711) 收329.5元，跌4.22%。

記憶體南亞科 (2408) 收223元，跌4.7%；華邦電 (2344) 收107.5元，跌2.27%；旺宏 (2337) 收148.5元，大跌7.19%；力積電 (6770) 收69.1元，大跌8.48%；記憶體模組廠威剛 (3260) 、創見 (2451) 、宇瞻 (8271) 弱勢跌停。

IC載板廠景碩 (3189) 收346元，大跌8.95%；欣興 (3037) 鎖跌停499元。

航運股長榮 (2603) 收204元，跌4.67%；萬海 (2615) 收74.8元，跌5.32%；陽明 (2609) 收52.8元，跌幅5.88%。

台新投顧副總經理黃文清分析，近期中東戰事訊息變化反覆，國際股市和台股波動明顯，儘管短期國際原油和天然氣價格高檔，不過中長期來看，多頭格局並未改變，台股短線走勢震盪整理。

展望台股後市，黃文清分析，若中東衝突局勢緩解不推延至下半年，國際油價有機會回檔，美國聯邦準備理事會（Fed）利率動向可望更為明確，科技股可延續反映未來估值。

台新主流基金經理人黃千雲指出，短期內全球發生數起重要事件，包括中東衝突引發地緣政治風險升溫等，主要影響市場氛圍，實際影響不大；若中東戰事儘早落幕，避險情緒應可逐漸消退，盤勢將回歸基本面，未來持續觀察中東戰爭變數是否衝擊全球經濟。

觀察台股AI相關供應鏈，黃千雲預期持續強勁成長，AI應用擴大走向邊緣運算端，AI相關零組件升級至少仍有2年至3年趨勢，北美雲端服務供應商（CSP）今年資本支出維持強勁，AI算力供不應求，產業仍處於營運上修階段，提供台股支撐點。（編輯：張良知）1150323

• 成交值量縮為新台幣6754.88億元。
• 電子指數跌2.5%，金融指數跌1.5%；櫃買OTC指數挫跌3.47%。
• 權王台積電終場收1810元，下跌30元，跌幅1.63%。
• 記憶體族群弱勢，力積電大跌8.48%，旺宏大跌7.19%，威剛、創見、宇瞻弱勢跌停。

news.Title: 台股狂殺千點！法人分析2大主因：短期波動加劇
news.Content:
【陳靜文／綜合報導】美股4大指數20日重挫，台股今(23日)開盤崩跌逾千點，一度下跌1082點，暫報32,461.09，成交金額僅362億元。權值股台積電一度下跌55元，暫報1785元。法人分析，台股今天開盤技術面震盪，在月線約33858點和季線約31600點之間波動加劇，後續仍要留意中東局勢及國際油價變化。

中東地區戰火升溫進入第4週，美國總統川普3月21日表示，若伊朗不在48小時內全面開放荷莫茲海峽（Strait of Hormuz），美國就會打擊並摧毀伊朗多座發電廠，會從最大電廠開始。伊朗軍方22日回應，若美方對電廠的威脅付諸實施，德黑蘭當局將採取徹底封鎖該水道等措施。

美伊衝突及能源危機升高，美股4大指數20日重挫，台積電ADR跌2.8%，台指期夜盤重挫822點收32737點，台積電期貨夜盤下跌35點收1815點。台股今(23日)開盤崩跌逾千點，一度下跌1082點，權值股台積電一度下跌55元。

根據中央社報導，證券法人分析，美伊衝突牽動國際原油價格上漲波動明顯，市場預期美國聯邦準備理事會（Fed）利率動向有變，貴金屬市場價格下跌，中東戰事不確定性和高油價波動時間拉長，影響市場預期心理，短期股市波動加劇。

期貨法人指出，法人或大型資金近日在台股指數高檔區間風險控管，留意籌碼面融資餘額已在新台幣4000億元高水位區間，此外全球政經局勢變化和能源價格上揚對通膨壓力影響，牽動美國聯準會利率決策動向、以及對半導體和電子族群估值表現。
